name: A.G. Holder Alert
subject: Did you see what Attorney General Holder said on MSNBC?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the National Democratic Redistricting Committee, John Bisognano, President
body:
Make a donation to fight for democracy and a fair electoral system
| [](https://click.everyaction.com/k/106033484/540203003/-3547583?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk0IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjE5MTBjOTExLWU4MWItZjAxMS04YjNkLTAwMjI0ODJhOWZiNyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=uWxVEDpLcFYPRjsZ9Y0Sv7C0uhR1L5bAS-CFSNYC_nk=&emci=13960938-971b-f011-8b3d-0022482a9fb7&emdi=1910c911-e81b-f011-8b3d-0022482a9fb7&ceid=8431045)
---

Peter, Recently, Attorney General Eric Holder appeared on MSNBC’s _All In with Chris Hayes_. He talked about our big win in Wisconsin to protect the state supreme court’s pro-democracy majority — and he laid out how we must defeat Trump’s extremist agenda across the country. **_“I think what happened in Wisconsin reverberates beyond the borders of that state and was, as Elon Musk said, a referendum on the Trump-Musk alliance and what it is that they have been doing to our country as they try to reorder our society.”

“And what people in Wisconsin said is that no, we're not going to put up with that. We're not going to first let you buy a seat for their preferred candidate on the Supreme Court. And second, that we're not in concert with or not supportive of what you're doing to our country.” _ ** _**— A.G. Eric Holder**_ [**Donate now to power more victories for our democracy.**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/106033485/540203004/1925646595?refcode=don_ndrc_tier1_email_AGonMSNBC_email_20250417-NDRC-Tier1-AGonMSNBC_125&utm_source=email&utm_medium=email&utm_term=don&utm_content=20250417-NDRC-Tier1-AGonMSNBC_125&utm_campaign=AGonMSNBC&amount=30&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk0IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjE5MTBjOTExLWU4MWItZjAxMS04YjNkLTAwMjI0ODJhOWZiNyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=uWxVEDpLcFYPRjsZ9Y0Sv7C0uhR1L5bAS-CFSNYC_nk=) Let’s be clear: Elon Musk poured more than $20 million into the Wisconsin Supreme Court race to try to buy an extreme right-wing court majority, which, as the GOP candidate stated, would act as a “support network” for Trump — and Wisconsinites stopped him. **But the threat goes far beyond the borders of the Badger State.** Trump and MAGA Republicans have made it clear they have no inclination to protect the American people. They would rather weaponize the law for their own selfish gains, to further their extremist agenda, all at the expense of working-class people. **We also know that the far right will redouble their efforts after losing in Wisconsin.** So we’ll need to keep stopping them at the ballot box this year, from Michigan to Pennsylvania to Virginia. Critical state-level races will continue to decide who controls the redistricting process and the levers of power. Ensuring free and fair elections is especially pressing right now because, as the GOP continues down their radical path, they will still try to cling to power by restricting voting rights and manipulating electoral maps. At the NDRC, we’re fighting back against these assaults on our freedoms, working to elect Democracy Defenders, and pushing forward with our 10 Year Plan to end gerrymandering and secure fair maps. Peter, we must treat this moment with the urgency it requires. [**Please rush a donation of $30 or more to fight for a fair electoral system and help ensure the will of the people is heard.**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/106033486/540203005/1925646595?refcode=don_ndrc_tier1_email_AGonMSNBC_email_20250417-NDRC-Tier1-AGonMSNBC_125&utm_source=email&utm_medium=email&utm_term=don&utm_content=20250417-NDRC-Tier1-AGonMSNBC_125&utm_campaign=AGonMSNBC&amount=30) | _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_

clean_body: Make a donation to fight for democracy and a fair electoral system | Peter, Recently, Attorney General Eric Holder appeared on MSNBC’s All In with Chris Hayes. He talked about our big win in Wisconsin to protect the state supreme court’s pro-democracy majority — and he laid out how we must defeat Trump’s extremist agenda across the country. “I think what happened in Wisconsin reverberates beyond the borders of that state and was, as Elon Musk said, a referendum on the Trump-Musk alliance and what it is that they have been doing to our country as they try to reorder our society.” “And what people in Wisconsin said is that no, we're not going to put up with that. We're not going to first let you buy a seat for their preferred candidate on the Supreme Court. And second, that we're not in concert with or not supportive of what you're doing to our country.” — A.G. Eric Holder = Let’s be clear: Elon Musk poured more than $20 million into the Wisconsin Supreme Court race to try to buy an extreme right-wing court majority, which, as the GOP candidate stated, would act as a “support network” for Trump — and Wisconsinites stopped him. But the threat goes far beyond the borders of the Badger State. Trump and MAGA Republicans have made it clear they have no inclination to protect the American people. They would rather weaponize the law for their own selfish gains, to further their extremist agenda, all at the expense of working-class people. We also know that the far right will redouble their efforts after losing in Wisconsin. So we’ll need to keep stopping them at the ballot box this year, from Michigan to Pennsylvania to Virginia. Critical state-level races will continue to decide who controls the redistricting process and the levers of power. Ensuring free and fair elections is especially pressing right now because, as the GOP continues down their radical path, they will still try to cling to power by restricting voting rights and manipulating electoral maps. At the NDRC, we’re fighting back against these assaults on our freedoms, working to elect Democracy Defenders, and pushing forward with our 10 Year Plan to end gerrymandering and secure fair maps. Peter, we must treat this moment with the urgency it requires. | If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: = | = = | = = | = Thank you, The NDRC Team = = Since 2017, the NDRC has executed a comprehensive redistricting strategy that shifts the redistricting power, creating fair districts where Democrats can compete. Our victories have been made possible by our strong community of grassroots supporters. = If you want to receive periodic updates from the NDRC on our fight for fair maps, text MAPS to 36787. Text HELP for help, STOP to end. Msg & Data rates may apply. We know we send a lot of emails, but we think it's important to keep you updated with the latest information on Republican gerrymandering and voter suppression and how we're fighting back -- regardless of whether you choose to donate. = to make a donation. name: Voter Action Project HQ
subject: “This is an attempt to destroy American democracy as we know it.”
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Integrity First Voter Action Project, www
body:
Sean Wilentz, a professor of American History at Princeton University, makes the stakes of the SAVE act clear: “ It’s the most extraordinary attack on voting rights in American history .”
We MUST stop this bill in the Senate and more like it but we need to unite and fight now, before opponents of voting rights in Congress start checking voter suppression bills off their to-do list.
RUSH $25 or more right now to stop the SAVE Act, defend our right to vote, and stop opponents of voting rights from destroying our democracy >> [https://secure.everyaction.com/pdjfDAEu4UCpXPQPEnrW-A2]
Voter Action Project [https://secure.everyaction.com/Qyv_-_BiF0iduOUt5sZ0yw2?am=20.00&contactdata=zvEaCO3GsF9HwiaCjum3lYB/zRFw7mkjjgWJHd73S1G4Cp2LyimO82oUw8KGCLqb9WCDtYl5T9gZmyTufb078kDHc0OtJENc3sCzqhtlhiI=]
CBS: Experts warn the proposed SAVE Act could make it harder for some married women to vote. Here's who could be affected. [https://secure.everyaction.com/pdjfDAEu4UCpXPQPEnrW-A2?am=20.00&contactdata=zvEaCO3GsF9HwiaCjum3lYB/zRFw7mkjjgWJHd73S1G4Cp2LyimO82oUw8KGCLqb9WCDtYl5T9gZmyTufb078kDHc0OtJENc3sCzqhtlhiI=]
The SAVE Act was fast-tracked by opponents of voting rights and passed the U.S. House of Representatives. This bill is a stunning assault on our fundamental right to vote, and we can not afford to let this anti-democratic legislation become law, folks.
Don’t be fooled by the name. This bill would force every voter to present proof of citizenship to register -- something millions of eligible Americans don’t have on hand. And that’s the point.
The bill would also obliterate popular methods of voter registration for all voters, including:
* Registration by mail

* 3rd party voter registration drives

* Online voter registration in states that allow it

* Automatic voter registration
Some estimates say that this bill could disenfranchise up to 11 million eligible Americans , especially women and those who live in under-resourced communities.
“ There’s never been an attack on voting rights out of Congress like this ,” said Alexander Keyssar, a professor of History and Social Policy at the Harvard Kennedy School, and a leading historian of voting rights. “ It’s always been the federal government trying to keep states in check on voting rights, for the most part. ”
Team, the consequences of a bill like this passing would be catastrophic for our democracy. Chip in with $25 or whatever you can today to stop the SAVE Act and other voter suppression bills from becoming law >> [https://secure.everyaction.com/pdjfDAEu4UCpXPQPEnrW-A2]
If you've saved your payment information with FastAction, your donation will go through immediately:
GIVE $15 NOW ➜ [https://secure.everyaction.com/pdjfDAEu4UCpXPQPEnrW-A2?am=15.00&tknfa=8bH7AuSqJvi1aybd0yG6V0EM50xncyOTjnr7FwvDQOA=&contactdata=zvEaCO3GsF9HwiaCjum3lYB/zRFw7mkjjgWJHd73S1G4Cp2LyimO82oUw8KGCLqb9WCDtYl5T9gZmyTufb078kDHc0OtJENc3sCzqhtlhiI=] GIVE $25 NOW ➜ [https://secure.everyaction.com/pdjfDAEu4UCpXPQPEnrW-A2?am=25.00&tknfa=8bH7AuSqJvi1aybd0yG6V0EM50xncyOTjnr7FwvDQOA=&contactdata=zvEaCO3GsF9HwiaCjum3lYB/zRFw7mkjjgWJHd73S1G4Cp2LyimO82oUw8KGCLqb9WCDtYl5T9gZmyTufb078kDHc0OtJENc3sCzqhtlhiI=] GIVE $50 NOW ➜ [https://secure.everyaction.com/pdjfDAEu4UCpXPQPEnrW-A2?am=50.00&tknfa=8bH7AuSqJvi1aybd0yG6V0EM50xncyOTjnr7FwvDQOA=&contactdata=zvEaCO3GsF9HwiaCjum3lYB/zRFw7mkjjgWJHd73S1G4Cp2LyimO82oUw8KGCLqb9WCDtYl5T9gZmyTufb078kDHc0OtJENc3sCzqhtlhiI=]

Integrity First Voter Action Project is dedicated to expanding ballot access and increasing voter participation across the country.
Since the Supreme Court gutted key provisions in the Voting Rights Act, extreme politicians have taken advantage to pass harsh laws that restrict access to the ballot box. These restrictions particularly affect disadvantaged communities and people of color. It’s our goal to bring awareness to these laws wherever they may crop up and organize voters to take action to ensure the right to vote is available to everyone in their states and communities.
Your support is crucial to our efforts. Will you donate today to help us protect voting rights? [https://secure.everyaction.com/Qyv_-_BiF0iduOUt5sZ0yw2]

name: Your Follow-Up [VPP]
subject: Waiting for your signature to send our letter!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by VPP
body:
Thank Jon Ossoff >>>
| Hi there, did you miss our email? We reached out with an exclusive opportunity to thank Sen. Jon Ossoff for leading the Anti-Elon Musk resistance!

**_However, your signature is still missing_ — so we wanted to give you one more chance to sign before we send the letter at 9am tomorrow.**

[**You can use this link or the button below to add your name! »**](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/89157?t=6&akid=19558%2E878179%2EHru31K) | [**THANK YOU JON OSSOFF: CHIP IN $3 TO SIGN »**](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/89157?t=7&akid=19558%2E878179%2EHru31K)
---
Thank you! The letter is attached for your reference.

The Voter Protection Project
**—BEGIN FORWARDED MESSAGE—**
TO: Senator Jon Ossoff
FROM: A proud supporter
Subject: I stand with you. Senator Ossoff,

__Thank you for all your hard work leading the Anti-Elon Musk resistance!__

It’s easy to feel helpless with MAGA Republicans in charge of the House, Senate, and White House, but you keep us hopeful that we _can_ fight back.

From fighting for Social Security to introducing a bill to guarantee the right to vote for all Americans, we KNOW we have someone in the Senate that we can ALWAYS count on to protect our rights.

**I know it isn’t easy fighting off Trump, Musk, and the GOP’s escalating threats to take you down, so please know that Democrats across the country stand with you.**

Your leadership is absolutely critical right now, and we hope more Democrats follow your lead.

[**I can’t thank you enough for all of your work to stop Donald Trump and Elon Musk from destroying our Democracy! So I’m signing this letter and chipping in $3 to the Voter Protection Project to show my gratitude and to keep electing incredible Democrats who will save our democracy from MAGA extremists like Donald Trump. »**](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/89157?t=8&akid=19558%2E878179%2EHru31K) | [**RUSH $3 TO SIGN ON BEFORE WE CLOSE THIS LETTER AT MIDNIGHT »**](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/89157?t=9&akid=19558%2E878179%2EHru31K)
---
**_X A grateful Democrat_**
\- The Voter Protection Project
| | Paid for by VPP
(www.protectvoting.org)
Not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. 401 2nd Ave. S, Ste. 303, Seattle, WA 98104

The Voter Protection Project is on an important mission: To end partisan gerrymandering and voter suppression by electing candidates at the state and federal levels that are forceful advocates for voting rights. Our enfranchisement agenda includes working to ensure that every state has automatic voter registration and online voter registration, convenient early voting, and independent redistricting commissions to ensure politicians are not able to draw their own maps.

Please, consider making a [one-time contribution by clicking here](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/121?t=1&akid=19558%2E878179%2EHru31K). **Or, to make the most powerful lasting impact and help us oust Republicans up and down the ballot who attack our right to vote, make a[monthly sustaining contribution by clicking here](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/122?t=2&akid=19558%2E878179%2EHru31K).**

[Click here](https://act.protectvoting.org/signup/fewer-emails/?t=3&akid=19558%2E878179%2EHru31K) to request fewer emails. Click here to [unsubscribe](https://act.protectvoting.org/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=10&akid=19558%2E878179%2EHru31K). | | [Please: Pitch In Once A Month To Protect Voting Rights »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/123?t=4&akid=19558%2E878179%2EHru31K)
---
[Donate Just One Time »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/124?t=5&akid=19558%2E878179%2EHru31K)

name: Johnny O
subject: What's it all for?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Johnny O for Congress
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/joc-2025?refcode=1.3 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/joc-2025?refcode=1.3]

Conservatives in Congress are making the largest cut to Medicaid in the program’s history. They’re gutting programs that put food on the table for hungry kids. They’re advancing bills to increase bank fees, slashing the federal workforce and eliminating entire federal agencies.
What’s it all for?
It’s not for you.
They aren’t taking any of these drastic measures to help stabilize the economy, lower our debt or put money back into the pockets of hard-working constituents. They’re doing it to help their billionaire friends get even richer.
Despite the reckless cuts, they’re adding trillions of dollars to the federal deficit to finance tax cuts worth $60,000 for the top 1 percent. Meanwhile, working families will get a couple hundred bucks that will be more than cancelled out by the Trump tariffs.
I won’t stand for it: I will do everything I can to help prevent this bad budget from becoming reality and to pass meaningful legislation for struggling families. Because, unlike my extreme colleagues, I’m in it for you.
CONTRIBUTE [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/joc-2025?refcode=1.3]

According to the latest reports, election experts say that House Democrats are on track to RETAKE THE HOUSE in the Midterm Elections. This just made our stomachs drop - here's why: 1\. AOC and Bernie Sanders are hosting massive rallies in key swing states

Make no mistake:

If we fail to catch up NOW, it's game over. House Democrats could seize control of the House and BLOCK everything President Trump tries to pass through Congress. They just jolted ahead in the fundraising race so now we're at a MAJOR disadvantage.

name: Laura Friedman
subject: I’m being targeted by Trump’s White House
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Laura Friedman for Congress
body:
| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/106034991/540242425/-319689629?refcode=em-header&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDM1MzYiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiZTZlNWM5ZWQtZjMxYi1mMDExLThiM2QtMDAyMjQ4MmE5ZmI3IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=Jaf9g2emMnPOvzoyBWRYLuDAhbXcRM8z5Zq0vYQiNlA=) Team, it looks like my recent town hall got under Donald Trump’s skin. **The White House rapid response team, which doubles as state-run propaganda, decided to put me “on blast” for acknowledging that I’m _doing my job_. ** [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/106034992/540242426/-319689629?refcode=em-4.17.25-all-a&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDM1MzYiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiZTZlNWM5ZWQtZjMxYi1mMDExLThiM2QtMDAyMjQ4MmE5ZmI3IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=Jaf9g2emMnPOvzoyBWRYLuDAhbXcRM8z5Zq0vYQiNlA=) This actually isn’t a secret... I’m part of the Democrats’ Litigation and Rapid Response Task Force aimed at stopping Trump’s most brazenly illegal and unconstitutional actions. They can find me every week if they’d like to. Our fight is transparent and public — unlike DOGE’s runaway cuts to lifesaving services. **I know the risks of opposing Trump and Musk’s illegal actions. The threat of retaliation by this administration is uniquely very real. ** **But in these moments, that cannot matter.** We are the equal mechanism our Constitution created to hold runaway power accountable. We won’t stop making sure this administration acts within the law. So yes, I will keep organizing no matter how many times the Trump administration and their online mobs come after me - because our Constitution matters. I’ve never let the right-wing attacks stop me, and I don’t plan on doing so now. But I can’t take them on alone. Nobody can. It’s going to take a movement. So I need to know if you’re with me. [**Friend, can you please chip in $3 or any amount to help me fight back against Trump, DOGE, and the authoritarians trying to take over our government? Any amount is appreciated; I’m just grateful to know you’re fighting alongside me.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/106034993/540242427/-319689629?refcode=em-4.17.25-all-a&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDM1MzYiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiZTZlNWM5ZWQtZjMxYi1mMDExLThiM2QtMDAyMjQ4MmE5ZmI3IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=Jaf9g2emMnPOvzoyBWRYLuDAhbXcRM8z5Zq0vYQiNlA=) __If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:__ | ****[$10](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/106034994/540242428/-319689629?refcode=em-4.17.25-all-a&express_lane=true&amount=10&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDM1MzYiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiZTZlNWM5ZWQtZjMxYi1mMDExLThiM2QtMDAyMjQ4MmE5ZmI3IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=Jaf9g2emMnPOvzoyBWRYLuDAhbXcRM8z5Zq0vYQiNlA=)**** | ****[$35](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/106034995/540242429/-319689629?refcode=em-4.17.25-all-a&express_lane=true&amount=35&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDM1MzYiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiZTZlNWM5ZWQtZjMxYi1mMDExLThiM2QtMDAyMjQ4MmE5ZmI3IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=Jaf9g2emMnPOvzoyBWRYLuDAhbXcRM8z5Zq0vYQiNlA=)****

Yesterday I wrote about the U.S. Government [building concentration camps in El Salvador](https://substack.com/redirect/c5a36419-d0b5-45b7-b55f-6b121b3786f1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), where they're placing legal residents like Kilmar Ábrego Garcia. Today I share that the Trump regime is now escalating their fascism by arresting and deporting U.S. citizens--including those innocent of any wrongdoing. This was never about safety. It was never about security. It was never about "law and order." It was always about advancing white supremacy. **Let 's Address This.**
**I am doing everything in my power to stave off fascism under this new administration. I cannot do it alone. Subscribe below and join the movement.**

---|---|---
Demonstrators rally during a status hearing in the Ábrego Garcia case in Greenbelt, Maryland on 15 April. Photograph: Annabelle Gordon/EPA
## Targeting U.S. Citizens
In Florida, 20-year-old Juan Carlos Lopez-Gomez was held in Leon County Jail. His crime? Allegedly entering the state as an "unauthorized alien." But here's the twist--Lopez-Gomez is a U.S. citizen. Born in the United States. He showed the judge his U.S. birth certificate. The judge didn't care. He remained in jail anyway with reports now emerging that he is to be released, finally.
In a healthy democracy, that should be front-page news. In a healthy democracy, judges don't detain citizens of the United States for having the wrong skin color or last name. But we are no longer in a healthy democracy. We are watching fascism unfold in real time.
And it gets worse.
ICE deported 19-year-old Merwil Gutierrez, a U.S. citizen, to a concentration camp in El Salvador. No crime. No charges. They knew he wasn't a target. They shipped him off anyway. This was done in February, and you're likely just now hearing about it for the first time. This is another example of the failure of corporate media.
Let me say that again: The United States government deported an innocent U.S. citizen to a foreign concentration camp.
And for those thinking, "Well, maybe he did something wrong..." I remind you that being _guilty_ of a crime doesn't justify human trafficking people to foreign torture camps either. The fact that we even need to say that out loud speaks volumes.
This is not a slippery slope. We are already at the bottom.
## Look At Who They Target--And Who They Don't
Let's take a step back.
* 1 out of every 7 Signers of the Declaration of Independence was an immigrant.
* 1 out of every 10 members of the First U.S. Congress was an immigrant.

Today? Only 1 out of 33 members of Congress is an immigrant. And the Trump regime is busy sending _more_ immigrants--to concentration camps.
But here's the point that should send a chill down your spine: They are no longer just targeting immigrants. They are targeting American citizens. If you are brown or Black in this country, your citizenship is not a shield. It is not respected. It is not real in the eyes of the fascists running this system.
And let's also talk about who they're _not_ targeting.
There are over 500,000 undocumented white immigrants in the United States. Canadians. Europeans. Some of them overstayed visas, some arrived without documentation at all. But when was the last time you saw ICE raiding their homes, schools, places of worship, or hospitals? You don't see Ron DeSantis holding them in jail. You don't see Trump scapegoating them at his rallies.
Why? Because they have what Trump and his ilk prize most: the complexion for protection.
## This Is What Fascism Looks Like
If you've been waiting for the big red flag--the moment when it's "official"--you've missed it. It already happened. This is what fascism looks like: the state weaponizing law enforcement against its own people, based not on what they've done, but on who they are. What starts with immigrants always ends with citizens. What starts at the border always spills into the streets.
They told you it was about "illegals."
Then it was about DACA recipients.
Then it was green a few card holders.
And now it's birthright citizens.
They are coming for Black and brown Americans next. Not for what they've done. But for what they look like.
**If you 're finding value in my advocacy, please invest by subscribing to help me raise our collective calls for justice.**
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..gW_eGXTRVoFAwmi9LVrQbhdB0oEL3rGVNAithJCDYh0?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=161564782)
## What Can We Do?
We are not helpless, and we can act. Some of the seven action items below have immediate effect and others take time. Some require financial investment, others require time investment. All of them matter and I ask you to do what you can.
### 1\. Activate
Get involved in your local politics. Whether it is your local school board, your local city council, or your local immigrant rights organization--get involved. Show up to the next meeting, meet your local elected officials, and understand the local issues impacting your community. Google is your friend in finding a local calendar of events. Use it. All politics is local, and it is critical you get involved locally.
### 2\. Communicate
Contact your member of Congress ([find them here](https://substack.com/redirect/cdfdde34-85c9-4cf6-ae15-a2b01c4d22a7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)) and relentlessly remind them that you do not support mass deportations. Instead, you do support due process of law, a humane immigration policy, and equal rights for immigrants. Ensure they hear your outrage as much as possible.
### 3\. Invest
Invest your time and your money into the people and organizations doing the front line work. If you have time to volunteer, make phone calls, knock doors, and sign petitions, do so. If you have the financial resources to donate to organizations working to protect our democracy, like the Chicago based [National Immigrant Justice Center](https://substack.com/redirect/1e695d47-e3d6-4a30-8fca-e61737242b84?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), please do so.
### 4\. Educate
It is critical we remain informed on the key issues, by educating ourselves and those around us. As corporate media sadly continues to capitulate to fascism, support writers and human rights lawyers working to stop fascism in America (e.g. [like yours truly](https://substack.com/redirect/0512835f-2e41-4d2a-90f8-cd628ceae616?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)). Read the work of non-profit media like ProPublica, The Appeal, and more. We may not have the billions of dollars in dark money from corporate autocrats, but we have strength in numbers and your support makes that difference.
### 5\. Organize
Bring friends with you to the April 19th nationwide peaceful protests. You are the best ambassador for justice in your friend group--lead by example. Grab even one friend and drag them with you to the next school board meeting, city council session, or town hall in your local community. We need to build organizational capacity, and means each one reaches one to get involved. (And when your friend comes to the second meeting, it is their opportunity to reach one more and bring them with).
### 6\. Sanctuary Locations
During the previous Trump administration, reports estimate 800 or more churches, synagogues, and other houses of worship established themselves as sanctuary locations for immigrants. These are locations that invited immigrants who feared deportation to remain within their walls for housing, food, and safety. To be clear, these sanctuary locations have no particular legal authority, and ICE agents can still procure a warrant to search sanctuary locations and arrest anyone a judge has authorized for arrest.
However, certain faith groups established sanctuary locations anyway as another line of protection and safety for those fearing such raids, which required ICE to have to make the decision of whether they want to violently raid a house of worship. And given the passing of the fascist Laken Riley Act, which allows authorities to arrest and deport immigrants on the mere accusation of a crime[3](https://substack.com/redirect/7109bcf6-cc66-4a14-ae8c-6af93079f302?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)--including petty and non-violent crimes like shoplifting--sanctuary locations are more critical than ever.
### 7\. Repeal 287g Agreements
The Illegal Immigration Reform and Immigrant Responsibility Act of 1996 added Section 287(g) to the Immigration and Nationality Act. This authorized ICE to force state and local police to do what ICE wants them to do--in this case, round up immigrants.[4](https://substack.com/redirect/57907306-b02d-4f30-9344-d4dec316acfd?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) In other words, 287g agreements allow federal ICE officers to require local and state police officers to work for them. These agreements are why it's incredibly risky to call local police on immigration issues. Currently, ICE has some form of a 287g agreement with hundreds of police departments across 25 states--[which I have uploaded here](https://substack.com/redirect/0f83fe15-f509-4185-af2f-3b6caf36cf96?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) for your convenience.
Here's the key call to action: 287g agreements cannot go into effect until local government approves their enforcement. That means, if your locality has a 287g agreement in place, it is because your local elected officials voted for it. Therefore, they can be repealed with that same local authority. So, if you live in one of the states or localities listed above, advocate to your city council, mayor, or relevant governing authority to repeal this harmful agreement and instead uphold meaningful justice.
## Conclusion
This is not the moment to be polite. This is not the moment to play politics. This is the moment to call it what it is: White supremacist fascism.
We must resist it with everything we've got--through the courts, through protest, through organizing, through storytelling, through solidarity. Because if we stay silent now, we give our consent. If we minimize now, we normalize. If we delay now, we may not get another chance.
They are coming for U.S. citizens. But the alarm should have sounded when they came for _anyone_ without due process of law. Let this fact move you to action.
Because history is watching--and we will be judged by what we chose to do when they started disappearing our neighbors.
**I am doing everything in my power to stave off fascism in the United States, and I need your support. Subscribe and join more than 128,000 fellow activists.**

Trump's fascist policies have already ensnared several U.S. citizens into jail and death camps--and horrifyingly it appears clear it will get worse | | | Apr 18 | | | | | | | | | | | | Yesterday I wrote about the U.S. Government , where they're placing legal residents like Kilmar Ábrego Garcia. Today I share that the Trump regime is now escalating their fascism by arresting and deporting U.S. citizens--including those innocent of any wrongdoing. This was never about safety. It was never about security. It was never about "law and order." It was always about advancing white supremacy. Let 's Address This. I am doing everything in my power to stave off fascism under this new administration. I cannot do it alone. Subscribe below and join the movement. | | ---|---|--- Demonstrators rally during a status hearing in the Ábrego Garcia case in Greenbelt, Maryland on 15 April. Photograph: Annabelle Gordon/EPA Targeting U.S. Citizens In Florida, 20-year-old Juan Carlos Lopez-Gomez was held in Leon County Jail. His crime? Allegedly entering the state as an "unauthorized alien." But here's the twist--Lopez-Gomez is a U.S. citizen. Born in the United States. He showed the judge his U.S. birth certificate. The judge didn't care. He remained in jail anyway with reports now emerging that he is to be released, finally. In a healthy democracy, that should be front-page news. In a healthy democracy, judges don't detain citizens of the United States for having the wrong skin color or last name. But we are no longer in a healthy democracy. We are watching fascism unfold in real time. And it gets worse. ICE deported 19-year-old Merwil Gutierrez, a U.S. citizen, to a concentration camp in El Salvador. No crime. No charges. They knew he wasn't a target. They shipped him off anyway. This was done in February, and you're likely just now hearing about it for the first time. This is another example of the failure of corporate media. Let me say that again: The United States government deported an innocent U.S. citizen to a foreign concentration camp. And for those thinking, "Well, maybe he did something wrong..." I remind you that being guilty of a crime doesn't justify human trafficking people to foreign torture camps either. The fact that we even need to say that out loud speaks volumes. This is not a slippery slope. We are already at the bottom. **_Co-Pilot Update: John James Enters the Race for Governor with overwhelming statewide and national support. _** Friend, Last week, John James officially announced his candidacy for Governor of Michigan. His announcement was met with a **surge of support from Michiganders across the state** — from auto workers and small business owners to families and grassroots leaders who are ready for new leadership in Lansing. The calls, social media posts and conversations all over Michigan have been incredible. [**_Read John’s Announcement Statement HERE._**](http://links.jjformichigan.com/u/click?_t=70fde66bc0554ca7a14c16f5ebafdbd9&_m=a152690b3ac340b8886f99628df8db28&_e=5J3G-3I9PnH_PcF-9svFc1ULi6cIgP9qiV0l5ki97JOdZl6PbzuOp5Eddd6AApmCbwnucHLwNsMPi5zEYkAe9WXL4vk3VWxgYiT_OIl6JAnGbgHbs-OIPoDPr56kJnDXKgbFgaevKelnnRQWmGmMELFVpYZjtKOzr-k12uAS0ZCAya6Fz-l0yLKR76oX7TIHNlPQi4yHwMnNdF916zzZxq9C6NPMvlqxblBD5a13_5o9onKuM6P0zaMkcs7OwebHCqN1ZCLOhA8dZ-Ok48fokGgUW_qrQzVFMsYX6lCn4r6DJfRLMjgs5wcP_e7rfv8K8WEKK71AqZuLsMBgQM2Vfw%3D%3D) [**_CLICK HERE to become a Co-Pilot on John's mission to take back the Governor’s Office._**](http://links.jjformichigan.com/u/click?_t=70fde66bc0554ca7a14c16f5ebafdbd9&_m=a152690b3ac340b8886f99628df8db28&_e=5J3G-3I9PnH_PcF-9svFc2P2wlCSxkcbiCYsm2rCOeB_px4DX83nxY2d9r-POQEHx-OtCvhkjGcSD-xSkGdNI-_f4hT-azqVLzCNyhIIeuBk4cxhHIySuYzW6BE5zDKYgIvPUU3VA8gvJAPKzVhA_lz9FQhrLGKr90h-ySSLJDPvWbK962iMv5heXuqnsLFKsQOilSdHx5YyGMuUCcKt1bXwwPRbc2icAMxQiuRdPzU%3D)

His statement highlights John’s leadership on military and defense issues critical to Michigan’s economy and our national security. _**_John’s Message: Michigan Deserves Better_**_ Also last week, John joined **_Newsmax’s National Report_** to outline his vision for Michigan. Michigan Democrats have spent the last 6 years trying to ruin everything that makes Michigan the best state in the nation to raise a family. We deserve better! [**Watch the**** _Newsmax_****interview here.**](http://links.jjformichigan.com/u/click?_t=70fde66bc0554ca7a14c16f5ebafdbd9&_m=a152690b3ac340b8886f99628df8db28&_e=5J3G-3I9PnH_PcF-9svFcykFSxwLV82tC32Ue4_YIbh5cZGpT-PGOxwY_Q0mOYUkNhwYLYEJwAz72ugLrwjXApJ_6E9j5uDgBoNo30kjfhNDy5srRqrOMoF7VghBJkMiXieI2qO4bLsrDDpMgvUvJtEsRC5tQrPHiDLBd_krV2JPGMUS4IeSklTMocgwghIqSSZ8CungShTUYEgjzbw-EC5GrgvKwMn5cxikJJpDwu-Tpg1ZdNCA98TXYRkcaKFu)

This is going to be a marathon, not a sprint. In case you aren’t up to date on the situation surrounding Kilmar Abrego Garcia, here’s what’s been going on:
* Last month, the Trump administration deported a man to an El Salvadoran prison.
* Since then, they’ve admitted that they deported him due to an “administrative error.”
* Recently, the Supreme Court ruled that the deportation was illegal and required that the US facilitate his return.
* **Trump and his cronies have ignored that ruling.**

Isn't it terrifying that Trump can erroneously deport people and suddenly they can never come back? How soon until Trump starts coming after American citizens or his political enemies and there’s no way to stop him? _We need to know what you think: Are we heading toward a Constitutional crisis, or do you think we are already in on?_ **[Do you think we are already in a Constitutional Crisis?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/106035205/540244380/-1631322539?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzUvMS84MDk5NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyZDQxMzRkMC1mOTFiLWYwMTEtOGIzZC0wMDIyNDgyYTlmYjciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Lgquokui9UK4aEL93iAag5xdwL_Ve_qzfUU2FGlzwgo=&emci=015b325c-ad1b-f011-8b3d-0022482a9fb7&emdi=2d4134d0-f91b-f011-8b3d-0022482a9fb7&ceid=560478)** [YES >>](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/106035206/540244381/-1631322539?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzUvMS84MDk5NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyZDQxMzRkMC1mOTFiLWYwMTEtOGIzZC0wMDIyNDgyYTlmYjciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Lgquokui9UK4aEL93iAag5xdwL_Ve_qzfUU2FGlzwgo=&emci=015b325c-ad1b-f011-8b3d-0022482a9fb7&emdi=2d4134d0-f91b-f011-8b3d-0022482a9fb7&ceid=560478) [NO >>](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/106035207/540244382/-1631322539?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzUvMS84MDk5NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyZDQxMzRkMC1mOTFiLWYwMTEtOGIzZC0wMDIyNDgyYTlmYjciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Lgquokui9UK4aEL93iAag5xdwL_Ve_qzfUU2FGlzwgo=&emci=015b325c-ad1b-f011-8b3d-0022482a9fb7&emdi=2d4134d0-f91b-f011-8b3d-0022482a9fb7&ceid=560478) Thanks for acting on this,

clean_body: Have you been following the news? | = | Have you been following the news around Kilmar Abrego Garcia? This is the man who the Trump Administration “accidentally” flew out of the country to a notoriously violent El Salvador prison who they claim we can’t get back! If this situation wasn’t dark enough... Now Donald Trump is blatantly ignoring the rulings of the judicial branch to bring Kilmar Abrego Garcia back. This a complete disregard for the constitutional separation of powers and checks and balances. With so many different opinions on this topic, we want to know your opinion: In case you aren’t up to date on the situation surrounding Kilmar Abrego Garcia, here’s what’s been going on: Last month, the Trump administration deported a man to an El Salvadoran prison. Since then, they’ve admitted that they deported him due to an “administrative error.” Recently, the Supreme Court ruled that the deportation was illegal and required that the US facilitate his return. Trump and his cronies have ignored that ruling. Isn't it terrifying that Trump can erroneously deport people and suddenly they can never come back? How soon until Trump starts coming after American citizens or his political enemies and there’s no way to stop him? We need to know what you think: Are we heading toward a Constitutional crisis, or do you think we are already in on? Thanks for acting on this, \- CADEM | | = Our Democratic leaders are working hard to make California a better place to live. From fighting for good-paying jobs, combating climate change, to expanding healthcare access so all Californians can lead safe, healthy, prosperous lives — California’s Democratic leaders turn progressive ideas into meaningful action. clean_body: Oregon Democrats Went Too Far Again! | | | | | | Friend, this is OUTRAGEOUS. Oregon Democrats just dropped a radical tax scheme that would force YOU to pay for their billion-dollar failures. Not a typo – they want to raise $2 BILLION every two years with a flood of new taxes and fees. They LOST track of $1 BILLION at ODOT. They BOTCHED major projects. They IGNORED warnings, accountability, and basic budgeting. Now they want to SLAM YOU with a dozen new taxes to cover their mess. | | | | | We aren’t talking about a few small changes. We’re talking about a full-blown assault on Oregon’s middle class. But Republicans are sounding the alarm. House Leader Christian Drazan called it a “slap in the face.” Rep. Shelly Boshart-Davis EXPOSEDODOT’s mismanagement and waste. Polls show 80% of Oregonians OPPOSEthese tax hikes. But the Democrats don’t care - instead, they’re doubling down. This is a defining moment. Either we sit back while Democrats bleed this state dry – or we FIGHT BACK and make them pay for the disaster they created. | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | Enough with the lies. * **TOP NEWS**

"**In historic first, Texas House approves private school voucher program,** " Texas Tribune's **Jaden Edison** and **Jasper Scherer** \-- "The Texas House gave initial approval early Thursday to a bill that would create a $1 billion private school voucher program, crossing a historic milestone and bringing Gov. **Greg Abbott 's** top legislative priority closer than ever to reaching his desk.

The lower chamber signed off on its voucher proposal, [Senate Bill 2](https://substack.com/redirect/f05f67ac-7121-41b7-a46c-8ad6dbc92e78?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), on an 85-63 vote. Every present Democrat voted against the bill. They were joined by two Republicans -- far short of the bipartisan coalitions that in previous legislative sessions consistently blocked proposals to let Texans use taxpayer money to pay for their children's private schooling.

"This is an extraordinary victory for the thousands of parents who have advocated for more choices when it comes to the education of their children," Abbott said in a statement, vowing that he would "swiftly sign this bill into law" when it reached his desk.
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $7/mo](https://substack.com/redirect/cf63226f-d4e7-4f3d-9050-5e845a009008?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
The vote came more than 10 hours after the chamber gave preliminary approval to its sweeping $7.7 billion school funding package, which would give local districts more money per student and raise teacher salaries. [House Bill 2](https://substack.com/redirect/887e7b5d-9267-48bc-a812-ff4f6bb851e5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), which passed on a 144-4 vote, also aims to improve the quality of special education services by allocating funding based on the [individual needs](https://substack.com/redirect/aad52425-4390-40df-b97a-02bacd1d2516?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) of children with disabilities.

Democrats argued the funding boost barely scratches the surface of what districts need to come back from budget deficits or to cover growing costs after years of inflation, but they ultimately supported the bill after a few hours of debate.
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $70/yr](https://substack.com/redirect/cf63226f-d4e7-4f3d-9050-5e845a009008?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
The more dramatic showdown came over the voucher bill, which Democrats tried to thwart with an amendment that would have put school vouchers up for a statewide vote in November. But the last-ditch maneuver attracted support from only one Republican -- Rep. **Dade Phelan** of Beaumont, the former House speaker -- spelling the demise of Democrats' one major play to derail the bill.

The landmark voucher vote marks the first time since 1957 that the Texas House has approved legislation making state money available for families to use on their children's private schooling. The outcome validated Abbott's crusade to build a pro-voucher House majority during last year's primary by targeting Republicans who tanked his previous proposal in 2023. Now, all that is left is for Republicans in both chambers to iron out the differences between their voucher plans, leaving Abbott and his allies on the brink of victory.

The House's plan would put $1 billion to create education savings accounts, a form of vouchers that families could use to pay for private school tuition and other school-related expenses, like textbooks, transportation and therapy. The bill would tie the voucher program's per-student dollars to public education funding so the amount available to each participating student would increase when public schools receive more money and dip when public education funding declines.

If public demand exceeds the program's capacity, students with disabilities and families defined by House lawmakers as low income would be prioritized -- though they would not be guaranteed admission to any private school.

Democrats expressed disappointment over the House's approval of vouchers, saying the outcome represented big money interests prevailing over those of everyday Texans.

"This bill is everything that is wrong with politics," said Rep. **Gina Hinojosa** , D-Austin.

After Thursday's vote, the House will still need to cast a final vote to approve both the voucher and school spending bills, largely a formality. The measures would then head to the Senate. At that point, members from both chambers would work to reconcile the differences in their voucher proposals in a closed-door conference committee. The biggest differences center on how much money participating students should receive, which applicants should take priority and how the program should accommodate students with disabilities.

The House debate on vouchers started Wednesday afternoon and ended early Thursday. Lawmakers changed a provision in the bill that would have limited funding for people without disabilities or from wealthier households -- defined as a family of four making about $156,000 or greater -- to only 20% of the program's total budget until after the 2026-27 school year. The 20% cap would now apply to each year of the potential voucher program.

The bill now also requires private schools to have existed for at least two years before joining the program; grants the state auditor more power to review the activities of organizations contracted to administer the program; and requires the state's annual report on the program to include dropout, expulsion and graduation data on participating students with disabilities -- broken down by grade, age, sex and race or ethnicity.

Wednesday's debate over SB 2 covered many of the talking points for and against vouchers echoed throughout the legislative session.

Republicans sought to assure their colleagues that the bill would prioritize low-income children and students with disabilities. Democrats noted that the legislation imposes no admission requirements on private schools, meaning they can deny any student, even those the state wants first in line for the program.

[Higher-income families](https://substack.com/redirect/23930145-f3fd-4f90-ae15-0f560c37856c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) whose children were [already attending private schools](https://substack.com/redirect/e83d580b-9f78-4f5e-9d0e-77a33139e655?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) have primarily benefited from the large-scale voucher programs enacted in other states." TOP NEWS "In historic first, Texas House approves private school voucher program, " Texas Tribune's Jaden Edison and Jasper Scherer \-- "The Texas House gave initial approval early Thursday to a bill that would create a $1 billion private school voucher program, crossing a historic milestone and bringing Gov. Greg Abbott 's top legislative priority closer than ever to reaching his desk. The lower chamber signed off on its voucher proposal, , on an 85-63 vote. Every present Democrat voted against the bill. They were joined by two Republicans -- far short of the bipartisan coalitions that in previous legislative sessions consistently blocked proposals to let Texans use taxpayer money to pay for their children's private schooling. "This is an extraordinary victory for the thousands of parents who have advocated for more choices when it comes to the education of their children," Abbott said in a statement, vowing that he would "swiftly sign this bill into law" when it reached his desk. The vote came more than 10 hours after the chamber gave preliminary approval to its sweeping $7.7 billion school funding package, which would give local districts more money per student and raise teacher salaries. , which passed on a 144-4 vote, also aims to improve the quality of special education services by allocating funding based on the of children with disabilities. Democrats argued the funding boost barely scratches the surface of what districts need to come back from budget deficits or to cover growing costs after years of inflation, but they ultimately supported the bill after a few hours of debate. The more dramatic showdown came over the voucher bill, which Democrats tried to thwart with an amendment that would have put school vouchers up for a statewide vote in November. But the last-ditch maneuver attracted support from only one Republican -- Rep. Dade Phelan of Beaumont, the former House speaker -- spelling the demise of Democrats' one major play to derail the bill. The landmark voucher vote marks the first time since 1957 that the Texas House has approved legislation making state money available for families to use on their children's private schooling. The outcome validated Abbott's crusade to build a pro-voucher House majority during last year's primary by targeting Republicans who tanked his previous proposal in 2023. Now, all that is left is for Republicans in both chambers to iron out the differences between their voucher plans, leaving Abbott and his allies on the brink of victory. The House's plan would put $1 billion to create education savings accounts, a form of vouchers that families could use to pay for private school tuition and other school-related expenses, like textbooks, transportation and therapy. The bill would tie the voucher program's per-student dollars to public education funding so the amount available to each participating student would increase when public schools receive more money and dip when public education funding declines. If public demand exceeds the program's capacity, students with disabilities and families defined by House lawmakers as low income would be prioritized -- though they would not be guaranteed admission to any private school. Democrats expressed disappointment over the House's approval of vouchers, saying the outcome represented big money interests prevailing over those of everyday Texans. "This bill is everything that is wrong with politics," said Rep. Gina Hinojosa , D-Austin. After Thursday's vote, the House will still need to cast a final vote to approve both the voucher and school spending bills, largely a formality. The measures would then head to the Senate. At that point, members from both chambers would work to reconcile the differences in their voucher proposals in a closed-door conference committee. The biggest differences center on how much money participating students should receive, which applicants should take priority and how the program should accommodate students with disabilities. The House debate on vouchers started Wednesday afternoon and ended early Thursday. Lawmakers changed a provision in the bill that would have limited funding for people without disabilities or from wealthier households -- defined as a family of four making about $156,000 or greater -- to only 20% of the program's total budget until after the 2026-27 school year. The 20% cap would now apply to each year of the potential voucher program. The bill now also requires private schools to have existed for at least two years before joining the program; grants the state auditor more power to review the activities of organizations contracted to administer the program; and requires the state's annual report on the program to include dropout, expulsion and graduation data on participating students with disabilities -- broken down by grade, age, sex and race or ethnicity. Wednesday's debate over SB 2 covered many of the talking points for and against vouchers echoed throughout the legislative session. Republicans sought to assure their colleagues that the bill would prioritize low-income children and students with disabilities. Democrats noted that the legislation imposes no admission requirements on private schools, meaning they can deny any student, even those the state wants first in line for the program. whose children were have primarily benefited from the large-scale voucher programs enacted in other states." "**In historic first, Texas House approves private school voucher program,** " Texas Tribune's **Jaden Edison** and **Jasper Scherer** \-- "The Texas House gave initial approval early Thursday to a bill that would create a $1 billion private school voucher program, crossing a historic milestone and bringing Gov. **Greg Abbott 's** top legislative priority closer than ever to reaching his desk.

The lower chamber signed off on its voucher proposal, [Senate Bill 2](https://substack.com/redirect/ddc4b0d5-ad42-45e8-960a-7dd5c10523cc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), on an 85-63 vote. The vote came more than 10 hours after the chamber gave preliminary approval to its sweeping $7.7 billion school funding package, which would give local districts more money per student and raise teacher salaries. [House Bill 2](https://substack.com/redirect/06c069ff-544c-464f-9c72-e7840e1c499c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), which passed on a 144-4 vote, also aims to improve the quality of special education services by allocating funding based on the [individual needs](https://substack.com/redirect/4069d465-6d40-4066-8581-f55f75388ec2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) of children with disabilities.

Democrats argued the funding boost barely scratches the surface of what districts need to come back from budget deficits or to cover growing costs after years of inflation, but they ultimately supported the bill after a few hours of debate.
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $70/yr](https://substack.com/redirect/4cdcbf24-bad2-497c-9ced-e235dbdc9338?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
The more dramatic showdown came over the voucher bill, which Democrats tried to thwart with an amendment that would have put school vouchers up for a statewide vote in November. But the last-ditch maneuver attracted support from only one Republican -- Rep. **Dade Phelan** of Beaumont, the former House speaker -- spelling the demise of Democrats' one major play to derail the bill.

The landmark voucher vote marks the first time since 1957 that the Texas House has approved legislation making state money available for families to use on their children's private schooling. The outcome validated Abbott's crusade to build a pro-voucher House majority during last year's primary by targeting Republicans who tanked his previous proposal in 2023. Now, all that is left is for Republicans in both chambers to iron out the differences between their voucher plans, leaving Abbott and his allies on the brink of victory.

The House's plan would put $1 billion to create education savings accounts, a form of vouchers that families could use to pay for private school tuition and other school-related expenses, like textbooks, transportation and therapy. The bill would tie the voucher program's per-student dollars to public education funding so the amount available to each participating student would increase when public schools receive more money and dip when public education funding declines.

If public demand exceeds the program's capacity, students with disabilities and families defined by House lawmakers as low income would be prioritized -- though they would not be guaranteed admission to any private school.

Democrats expressed disappointment over the House's approval of vouchers, saying the outcome represented big money interests prevailing over those of everyday Texans.

"This bill is everything that is wrong with politics," said Rep. **Gina Hinojosa** , D-Austin.

After Thursday's vote, the House will still need to cast a final vote to approve both the voucher and school spending bills, largely a formality. The measures would then head to the Senate. At that point, members from both chambers would work to reconcile the differences in their voucher proposals in a closed-door conference committee. The biggest differences center on how much money participating students should receive, which applicants should take priority and how the program should accommodate students with disabilities.

The House debate on vouchers started Wednesday afternoon and ended early Thursday. Lawmakers changed a provision in the bill that would have limited funding for people without disabilities or from wealthier households -- defined as a family of four making about $156,000 or greater -- to only 20% of the program's total budget until after the 2026-27 school year. The 20% cap would now apply to each year of the potential voucher program.

The bill now also requires private schools to have existed for at least two years before joining the program; grants the state auditor more power to review the activities of organizations contracted to administer the program; and requires the state's annual report on the program to include dropout, expulsion and graduation data on participating students with disabilities -- broken down by grade, age, sex and race or ethnicity.

Wednesday's debate over SB 2 covered many of the talking points for and against vouchers echoed throughout the legislative session.

Republicans sought to assure their colleagues that the bill would prioritize low-income children and students with disabilities. Democrats noted that the legislation imposes no admission requirements on private schools, meaning they can deny any student, even those the state wants first in line for the program.

[Higher-income families](https://substack.com/redirect/a4be35c6-1dd1-4ea9-8872-559902e623ed?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) whose children were [already attending private schools](https://substack.com/redirect/a908e734-7ea4-4b06-a2c9-32b62e6dee7e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) have primarily benefited from the large-scale voucher programs enacted in other states." [Texas Tribune](https://substack.com/redirect/c56e15a7-5245-49c3-a031-e5093eada229?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

TOP NEWS "In historic first, Texas House approves private school voucher program, " Texas Tribune's Jaden Edison and Jasper Scherer \-- "The Texas House gave initial approval early Thursday to a bill that would create a $1 billion private school voucher program, crossing a historic milestone and bringing Gov. Greg Abbott 's top legislative priority closer than ever to reaching his desk. The lower chamber signed off on its voucher proposal, , on an 85-63 vote. Every present Democrat voted against the bill. They were joined by two Republicans -- far short of the bipartisan coalitions that in previous legislative sessions consistently blocked proposals to let Texans use taxpayer money to pay for their children's private schooling. "This is an extraordinary victory for the thousands of parents who have advocated for more choices when it comes to the education of their children," Abbott said in a statement, vowing that he would "swiftly sign this bill into law" when it reached his desk. The vote came more than 10 hours after the chamber gave preliminary approval to its sweeping $7.7 billion school funding package, which would give local districts more money per student and raise teacher salaries. , which passed on a 144-4 vote, also aims to improve the quality of special education services by allocating funding based on the of children with disabilities. Democrats argued the funding boost barely scratches the surface of what districts need to come back from budget deficits or to cover growing costs after years of inflation, but they ultimately supported the bill after a few hours of debate. The more dramatic showdown came over the voucher bill, which Democrats tried to thwart with an amendment that would have put school vouchers up for a statewide vote in November. But the last-ditch maneuver attracted support from only one Republican -- Rep. Dade Phelan of Beaumont, the former House speaker -- spelling the demise of Democrats' one major play to derail the bill. The landmark voucher vote marks the first time since 1957 that the Texas House has approved legislation making state money available for families to use on their children's private schooling. The outcome validated Abbott's crusade to build a pro-voucher House majority during last year's primary by targeting Republicans who tanked his previous proposal in 2023. Now, all that is left is for Republicans in both chambers to iron out the differences between their voucher plans, leaving Abbott and his allies on the brink of victory. The House's plan would put $1 billion to create education savings accounts, a form of vouchers that families could use to pay for private school tuition and other school-related expenses, like textbooks, transportation and therapy. The bill would tie the voucher program's per-student dollars to public education funding so the amount available to each participating student would increase when public schools receive more money and dip when public education funding declines. If public demand exceeds the program's capacity, students with disabilities and families defined by House lawmakers as low income would be prioritized -- though they would not be guaranteed admission to any private school. Democrats expressed disappointment over the House's approval of vouchers, saying the outcome represented big money interests prevailing over those of everyday Texans. "This bill is everything that is wrong with politics," said Rep. Gina Hinojosa , D-Austin. After Thursday's vote, the House will still need to cast a final vote to approve both the voucher and school spending bills, largely a formality. The measures would then head to the Senate. At that point, members from both chambers would work to reconcile the differences in their voucher proposals in a closed-door conference committee. The biggest differences center on how much money participating students should receive, which applicants should take priority and how the program should accommodate students with disabilities. The House debate on vouchers started Wednesday afternoon and ended early Thursday. Lawmakers changed a provision in the bill that would have limited funding for people without disabilities or from wealthier households -- defined as a family of four making about $156,000 or greater -- to only 20% of the program's total budget until after the 2026-27 school year. The 20% cap would now apply to each year of the potential voucher program. The bill now also requires private schools to have existed for at least two years before joining the program; grants the state auditor more power to review the activities of organizations contracted to administer the program; and requires the state's annual report on the program to include dropout, expulsion and graduation data on participating students with disabilities -- broken down by grade, age, sex and race or ethnicity. Wednesday's debate over SB 2 covered many of the talking points for and against vouchers echoed throughout the legislative session. Republicans sought to assure their colleagues that the bill would prioritize low-income children and students with disabilities. Democrats noted that the legislation imposes no admission requirements on private schools, meaning they can deny any student, even those the state wants first in line for the program. whose children were have primarily benefited from the large-scale voucher programs enacted in other states." * **TOP NEWS**

"**In historic first, Texas House approves private school voucher program,** ” Texas Tribune's **Jaden Edison** and **Jasper Scherer** \-- "The Texas House gave initial approval early Thursday to a bill that would create a $1 billion private school voucher program, crossing a historic milestone and bringing Gov. **Greg Abbott’s** top legislative priority closer than ever to reaching his desk.

The lower chamber signed off on its voucher proposal, [Senate Bill 2](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.y1qQg7vX491TJbzrGLYTuEYT0yv_aZw1IceJac_-o0Y-UGIWKkPTFm9Kz5DRdvb_-aYU7NLaOywMgY5yceksY9DWMi5G6R03DGvEJyxx5SfsnqSm8utBaOjxj7L3V0QonPUv6BCzh6oHjFmI0ky7e59u_AIRgyCCKpPV6_ajOkZ4Jc4M3t0JeogwSbXWtKvI1B1-Br1awNWVnQ3AHb0Y5Ao34ZYWbSy35v3CMp2lyfh3nw-zEwwXxefprsN1LrLnwxPOcSWKAgXR18LQp4Rnrb3bBkhyPISq_1U0t22EakhokECJYYW8FaDitfIN3I-UMo59tpUzVZwqeX2TQQXq0BToHig3FqYg4n05KD24664wQdBhw1f_urVMxPG3zeaqwmpFipG0OpR_U1N6eI8HHo9uW2gznZcvEPB6Ddem47NVcDSdwyF18ekG4YxkAwXJTO72bHgFMgrpMv7wL_f2zw/4fr/HoFEAVAKRLWtTgFQUI7oAw/h11/h001.A-AJHMSzf0cpPx5KB60qVq0Tz3FNRq-yWA69KunWJ90), on an 85-63 vote. Every present Democrat voted against the bill. They were joined by two Republicans — far short of the bipartisan coalitions that in previous legislative sessions consistently blocked proposals to let Texans use taxpayer money to pay for their children’s private schooling.

“This is an extraordinary victory for the thousands of parents who have advocated for more choices when it comes to the education of their children,” Abbott said in a statement, vowing that he would “swiftly sign this bill into law” when it reached his desk.

Democrats argued the funding boost barely scratches the surface of what districts need to come back from budget deficits or to cover growing costs after years of inflation, but they ultimately supported the bill after a few hours of debate.

The more dramatic showdown came over the voucher bill, which Democrats tried to thwart with an amendment that would have put school vouchers up for a statewide vote in November. But the last-ditch maneuver attracted support from only one Republican — Rep. **Dade Phelan** of Beaumont, the former House speaker — spelling the demise of Democrats’ one major play to derail the bill.

The landmark voucher vote marks the first time since 1957 that the Texas House has approved legislation making state money available for families to use on their children’s private schooling. The outcome validated Abbott’s crusade to build a pro-voucher House majority during last year’s primary by targeting Republicans who tanked his previous proposal in 2023. Now, all that is left is for Republicans in both chambers to iron out the differences between their voucher plans, leaving Abbott and his allies on the brink of victory.

The House’s plan would put $1 billion to create education savings accounts, a form of vouchers that families could use to pay for private school tuition and other school-related expenses, like textbooks, transportation and therapy. The bill would tie the voucher program’s per-student dollars to public education funding so the amount available to each participating student would increase when public schools receive more money and dip when public education funding declines.

If public demand exceeds the program’s capacity, students with disabilities and families defined by House lawmakers as low income would be prioritized — though they would not be guaranteed admission to any private school.

Democrats expressed disappointment over the House’s approval of vouchers, saying the outcome represented big money interests prevailing over those of everyday Texans.

“This bill is everything that is wrong with politics,” said Rep. **Gina Hinojosa** , D-Austin.

After Thursday’s vote, the House will still need to cast a final vote to approve both the voucher and school spending bills, largely a formality. The measures would then head to the Senate. At that point, members from both chambers would work to reconcile the differences in their voucher proposals in a closed-door conference committee. The biggest differences center on how much money participating students should receive, which applicants should take priority and how the program should accommodate students with disabilities.

The House debate on vouchers started Wednesday afternoon and ended early Thursday. Lawmakers changed a provision in the bill that would have limited funding for people without disabilities or from wealthier households — defined as a family of four making about $156,000 or greater — to only 20% of the program’s total budget until after the 2026-27 school year. The 20% cap would now apply to each year of the potential voucher program.

The bill now also requires private schools to have existed for at least two years before joining the program; grants the state auditor more power to review the activities of organizations contracted to administer the program; and requires the state’s annual report on the program to include dropout, expulsion and graduation data on participating students with disabilities — broken down by grade, age, sex and race or ethnicity.

Wednesday’s debate over SB 2 covered many of the talking points for and against vouchers echoed throughout the legislative session.

Republicans sought to assure their colleagues that the bill would prioritize low-income children and students with disabilities. Democrats noted that the legislation imposes no admission requirements on private schools, meaning they can deny any student, even those the state wants first in line for the program.

Read the most recent FREE subscriber email. Become a PAID subscriber for $5/mo or $50/yr . | TOP NEWS "In historic first, Texas House approves private school voucher program, ” Texas Tribune's Jaden Edison and Jasper Scherer \-- "The Texas House gave initial approval early Thursday to a bill that would create a $1 billion private school voucher program, crossing a historic milestone and bringing Gov. Greg Abbott’s top legislative priority closer than ever to reaching his desk. The lower chamber signed off on its voucher proposal, , on an 85-63 vote. Every present Democrat voted against the bill. They were joined by two Republicans — far short of the bipartisan coalitions that in previous legislative sessions consistently blocked proposals to let Texans use taxpayer money to pay for their children’s private schooling. “This is an extraordinary victory for the thousands of parents who have advocated for more choices when it comes to the education of their children,” Abbott said in a statement, vowing that he would “swiftly sign this bill into law” when it reached his desk. » The vote came more than 10 hours after the chamber gave preliminary approval to its sweeping $7.7 billion school funding package, which would give local districts more money per student and raise teacher salaries. , which passed on a 144-4 vote, also aims to improve the quality of special education services by allocating funding based on the of children with disabilities. Democrats argued the funding boost barely scratches the surface of what districts need to come back from budget deficits or to cover growing costs after years of inflation, but they ultimately supported the bill after a few hours of debate. » The more dramatic showdown came over the voucher bill, which Democrats tried to thwart with an amendment that would have put school vouchers up for a statewide vote in November. But the last-ditch maneuver attracted support from only one Republican — Rep. Dade Phelan of Beaumont, the former House speaker — spelling the demise of Democrats’ one major play to derail the bill. The landmark voucher vote marks the first time since 1957 that the Texas House has approved legislation making state money available for families to use on their children’s private schooling. The outcome validated Abbott’s crusade to build a pro-voucher House majority during last year’s primary by targeting Republicans who tanked his previous proposal in 2023. Now, all that is left is for Republicans in both chambers to iron out the differences between their voucher plans, leaving Abbott and his allies on the brink of victory. The House’s plan would put $1 billion to create education savings accounts, a form of vouchers that families could use to pay for private school tuition and other school-related expenses, like textbooks, transportation and therapy. The bill would tie the voucher program’s per-student dollars to public education funding so the amount available to each participating student would increase when public schools receive more money and dip when public education funding declines. If public demand exceeds the program’s capacity, students with disabilities and families defined by House lawmakers as low income would be prioritized — though they would not be guaranteed admission to any private school. Democrats expressed disappointment over the House’s approval of vouchers, saying the outcome represented big money interests prevailing over those of everyday Texans. “This bill is everything that is wrong with politics,” said Rep. Gina Hinojosa , D-Austin. After Thursday’s vote, the House will still need to cast a final vote to approve both the voucher and school spending bills, largely a formality. The measures would then head to the Senate. At that point, members from both chambers would work to reconcile the differences in their voucher proposals in a closed-door conference committee. The biggest differences center on how much money participating students should receive, which applicants should take priority and how the program should accommodate students with disabilities. The House debate on vouchers started Wednesday afternoon and ended early Thursday. Lawmakers changed a provision in the bill that would have limited funding for people without disabilities or from wealthier households — defined as a family of four making about $156,000 or greater — to only 20% of the program’s total budget until after the 2026-27 school year. The 20% cap would now apply to each year of the potential voucher program. The bill now also requires private schools to have existed for at least two years before joining the program; grants the state auditor more power to review the activities of organizations contracted to administer the program; and requires the state’s annual report on the program to include dropout, expulsion and graduation data on participating students with disabilities — broken down by grade, age, sex and race or ethnicity. Wednesday’s debate over SB 2 covered many of the talking points for and against vouchers echoed throughout the legislative session. Republicans sought to assure their colleagues that the bill would prioritize low-income children and students with disabilities. Democrats noted that the legislation imposes no admission requirements on private schools, meaning they can deny any student, even those the state wants first in line for the program. whose children were have primarily benefited from the large-scale voucher programs enacted in other states." | FREE SUBSCRIPTIONS HAVE ENDED : As of July 11, 2022, we have moved to a paywall for free subscribers. name: Hillsdale College via ICYMI News
subject: Defeat DEI Survey
body:
DEI policies have infected almost every aspect of American life, from K-12 schools to college admissions and job applications.

This sponsored message from ICYMI News is on behalf of Hillsdale College
| | | [](http://e.icyminews.com/rd/9z2znsn3h6ke9968783u8it2hqtrao43k74ojgmf5ao_rp22sh2s8i6eob1cjjm0ob1c1h2bs)
---
| |
Dear Friend of Liberty,

[I'm writing to ask you to participate in Hillsdale College's **DEFEAT DEI SURVEY**](http://e.icyminews.com/rd/9z2z5r33821k46ifdtrjj1hf2k80j2gbicm3ofhddlo_rp22sh2s8i6eob1cjjm0ob1c1h2bs).

DEI policies have infected almost every aspect of American life, from K-12 schools to college admissions and job applications. They have even spread into the hard sciences and the military.

These policies endanger lives and, ultimately, Western Civilization itself.

I'm sure, like a lot of Americans, you want to know what you can do to stop DEI. One of the most fundamental steps is ...

**Countering the far-Left ideologies that are being taught to students in K-12 schools, colleges, and universities.**

Please join Hillsdale College in our defense of Western Civilization by taking your **DEFEAT DEI SURVEY** today. You can find it at this secure link:

[**https://info.hillsdale.edu/defeat-dei-survey**](http://e.icyminews.com/rd/9z2zhjc8e0nnbpr1r5ddj125sduah55fe262o2qrdro_rp22sh2s8i6eob1cjjm0ob1c1h2bs)

With your help, we will continue our work to defeat the tyranny of low standards and to restore excellence in America.

Warm regards,

Larry P. Arnn
President, Hillsdale College
Pursuing Truth and Defending Liberty Since 1844
