clean_body: | | Patriot, The Goldwater Institute is And it starts by getting Marxist propaganda OUT of our schools. We have only one chance to make that happen, and we need the help of freedom-loving Americans like you. Right now, one in four American public high schools teach history through the lens of one radical socialist: Howard Zinn. Zinn paints a distorted picture of America, defined by oppression, exploitation, and division. He ignores the values that made America a beacon of freedom and opportunity. That’s why our colleges are hotbeds of hate and anti-Americanism. That’s why so many young Americans see socialism as the “solution.” That’s why so many young Americans are choosing despair over hope. We are exposing this radicalism and giving parents the resources they need to ensure that America’s children learn our nation’s founding principles. As a service to parents and educators, the Goldwater Institute’s Van Sittert Center for Constitutional Advocacy compared two of the most ambitious and prominent literary projects ever undertaken to teach high schoolers the history of the United States of America: Howard Zinn’s A People’s History of the United States , and Wilfred McClay’s Land of Hope: An Invitation to the Great American Story — a textbook that gives students an honest, balanced view of both America’s virtues and struggles. Our report details to parents how the adoption of one text versus the other will determine which narrative about the American experiment future generations adopt. Rather than provoke division and distrust, we must help students engage with the nuanced history of the United States with both honesty and high expectations for the future. I am asking for your immediate help to win this battle for the minds of the next generation. Our nation depends on them, knowing America is a place where they can thrive and a force for good in the world. The Goldwater Institute is focusing on expanding liberty at the state level , where decisions that directly affect our families are made. We have SECURED high-impact reforms in all 50 states — and defended them tirelessly in court to make sure they actually work as promised: DEFENDING Parents’ Rights: Working with parents to help them wage the legal battles to STOP schools from hiding information about their kids. EXPANDING School Choice: Making Arizona the first state to offer education savings accounts, and helping other states like Florida, Mississippi, North Carolina, and Tennessee in waging similar battles for school choice. DEFEATING DEI: launching a new 50-state legislative push to outlaw political litmus tests in our universities, once and for all. And now, STOPPING the Leftist propaganda that’s infected the minds of our children and turned them against our nation’s values. The Goldwater Institute not only passes reforms — we defend them in court. When the government refuses to follow the law, our best-in-class legal team takes action to hold them accountable. Our strategy is simple: 1. Pass the law. 2. Defend the law. 3. Deliver results the American people are counting on. But high-stakes legal fights on 50 different battlefields aren’t cheap. And we can’t count on Hollywood, the media, or woke billionaires backing us. EVERY dollar you give today will directly advance and defend policies that protect liberty and save our schools from radical indoctrination. And EVERY amount makes an impact. Thank you for standing with us to protect the future of American education and this great nation. Sincerely, Victor Riches President & CEO Goldwater Institute The Goldwater Institute is recognized by the Internal Revenue Service as a 501(c)(3) organization. Our federal identification number is 86-0597661.

name: MAHA Action
subject: Welcome to MAHA, Na
body:
You stood alongside Robert F. Kennedy Jr. when he challenged the political establishment in Washington—and you never backed down.

|
---
| | | |
---
|
---
[](https://www.mahaaction.com?e=674c9b4e7ea0bed829630c80c355f23d&utm_source=maha&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=k24_drip_1_open_6m&n=2)
---
[](https://www.mahaaction.com/about?e=674c9b4e7ea0bed829630c80c355f23d&utm_source=maha&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=k24_drip_1_open_6m&n=3)
|
---
❤️🔥 Welcome to MAHA
You Helped Start a Revolution—Now Let’s Lead It
Na,
Thank you.
You stood alongside Robert F. Kennedy Jr. when he challenged the political establishment in Washington—**and you never backed down**.
You gave, you volunteered, you shared his message, and you stood up for radical change during deep uncertainty. You were part of a historic campaign to reclaim your health and freedom and restore accountability to our governing institutions.
As you know, RFK Jr.’s mission did not end on Election Day. _In fact, thanks to you, it is only just beginning_.
MAHA Action—**Make America Healthy Again** —is the next chapter of this movement.
Led by longtime health freedom advocate, former Team Kennedy Communications Director, and The HighWire host **Del Bigtree** , MAHA Action is a mission-driven, non-partisan 501(c)(4) built from the revolutionary grassroots movement that you helped ignite in 2024.
[](https://www.mahaaction.com/a-message-to-our-family?e=674c9b4e7ea0bed829630c80c355f23d&utm_source=maha&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=k24_drip_1_open_6m&n=4)
Our team—made up of former Team Kennedy superstars—is boots on the ground and laser-focused on delivering real reform, not more bureaucracy.
Now, with RFK Jr. leading the Department of Health and Human Services, we have a once-in-a-generation opportunity to transform our nation’s health policies from the inside out.
Empowering citizens to act.
We provide the tools everyday Americans need to make their voices heard—whether it’s tracking key health legislation, contacting elected officials, or advocating for reform at the local, state, and federal levels.
Our new [Legislative Tracker](https://www.mahaaction.com/lawmakers?e=674c9b4e7ea0bed829630c80c355f23d&utm_source=maha&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=k24_drip_1_open_6m&n=5) equips concerned citizens with real-time updates and engagement tools to influence the decisions that affect their families and communities.
You can also dive deeper into the movement through [The MAHA Podcast](https://www.mahaaction.com/r?u=1AJnCcoGtHYftrUTYBwtFJuNdrTF8w7HRCKLhthf7E4LDsvcrjQKkUjK7vkuBFbzNRrYq5CKiz19_itHHp0D3qd0wr3NZ5LqL4FnY1PKw4w&e=674c9b4e7ea0bed829630c80c355f23d&utm_source=maha&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=k24_drip_1_open_6m&n=6), where we feature thought leaders, health professionals, and everyday Americans sharing their stories and insights on how we reclaim health freedom and integrity in medicine.
Serving as a national watchdog.
MAHA Action is committed to uncovering what has long been hidden. Through Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) requests, legal action, and investigative oversight, we’re exposing the entrenched interests and regulatory failures that have eroded trust in our public health agencies.
Until the system is fully transformed, we will hold it accountable.
Mobilizing science, law, and grassroots energy.
Our work brings together doctors, researchers, legal experts, and citizen advocates to build a unified front for reform. From supporting RFK Jr.’s internal policy agenda to organizing public engagement efforts and educating the next generation of leaders, MAHA Action is coordinating the people-powered infrastructure needed to take back our health institutions.
MAHA Action represents a focused, strategic push to restore integrity, accountability, and truth to American healthcare—**and now, you’re part of that mission.**
[Learn More](https://www.mahaaction.com/about?e=674c9b4e7ea0bed829630c80c355f23d&utm_source=maha&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=k24_drip_1_open_6m&n=7)
With determination,
MAHA Action
|
---
[](https://www.mahaaction.com?e=674c9b4e7ea0bed829630c80c355f23d&utm_source=maha&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=k24_drip_1_open_6m&n=8)
Make America Healthy Again
|
---

body:
The Trump administration is ripping to shreds the foundations of our democracy.

| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/106094978/540405858/2116327045?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85ODI2MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlODI3MWNhYS1hNDFjLWYwMTEtOGIzZC0wMDIyNDgyYTlmYjciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=lE8ifA9u0KbW1N5dCkonDEWAgPAVxMfJYRn-RyFW2wQ=&emci=4d79c217-901c-f011-8b3d-0022482a9fb7&emdi=e8271caa-a41c-f011-8b3d-0022482a9fb7&ceid=6681226)
---
| Kilmar Abrego Garcia—a husband, a father, and a legal resident—was wrongfully deported by the Trump administration in clear defiance of a federal court order. Let me say that again: the courts ruled that Kilmar should stay. The administration ignored them and deported him anyway.

Not only is his wrongful deportation unjust to him and his family, but it’s in open defiance of our nation’s courts and a flagrant violation of our constitutional right to due process.

If a President can pick and choose which court orders to follow, what’s next? If a **legal resident** can be ripped from his home and loved ones despite court protection, who’s safe?

The Trump administration is ripping to shreds the foundations of our democracy. In our country, one of the underpinnings of our nation is that everyone is entitled to due process, citizen or not. This administration has disregarded all rights to freedom and due process.

It is **all** of our responsibility to speak out against this gross abuse of power. We must demand accountability.

Mr. Garcia must be brought home, and the Trump Administration must act now and ensure due process rights remain in place for all of us. In solidarity, Linda
---

* * *

Donald Trump has effectively "sold" Kilmar Abrego Garcia, a lawful asylum seeker, husband to a U.S. citizen, and father of three U.S. citizen children, into the hands of El Salvador's dictator. _This egregious act is a stark reminder of the perilous state of our democracy._ ICE has admitted to an "administrative error" in the rendition of Abrego Garcia, who has lived in the U.S. for 14 years under protected legal status and has committed no crime. _Yet, he has been unjustly thrust into a brutal Salvadoran prison, stripped of his right to due process._ A federal judge, recognizing the injustice, ordered the Trump administration to bring Abrego Garcia back to the U.S. However, Trump has blatantly refused, and El Salvador's dictator, Nayib Bukele, has brazenly declared he will not return him. **_This is NOT what democracy looks like!_****At Able Dems, we are dedicated to electing Democrats with disabilities and their allies who will fiercely defend our democracy and uphold our rights.** **[Will you join us and sign on to DEMAND that Congress bring these men who were denied due process We’re really proud of Josh. He’s battling in one of the most competitive seats in the country and also charting a path for Democrats on how to fight back and win. We need a vision - and Josh is helping lead our party towards it. More news to come in this space soon! Team Harder

name: Team Jayapal
subject: A party with a proposition – not just opposition
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Pramila for Congress
body:
At the Bernie rally, Pramila laid out exactly what we’re fighting for. Here’s what she said.
# [](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/go/892?refcode=em20250418-4963-footer&t=1&akid=4963%2E420207%2EmTEno3)
Peter, at the Bernie Sanders and AOC rally in Los Angeles, Pramila brought the house down with a message we all need to hear right now:
**We’re not just an opposition party – we’re a _proposition_ party.**
[](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/pj-email-20250418-1?refcode=em20250418-4963&amount=3&refcode2=4963_420207_mTEno3&akid=4963%2E420207%2EmTEno3)
While MAGA Republicans push a budget that slashes Medicaid and delivers massive tax breaks to billionaires, we’re fighting for something _better_ :
✅ Universal childcare that’s actually affordable
✅ A healthcare system that ensures _everyone_ can get the care they need without breaking the bank
✅ Fundamental freedoms, from the right to decide what happens to your own body to the right to speak your mind and voice your opinion
**This isn’t just about resisting the far-right. It’s about advancing a bold, inclusive vision for the future where working people come first, not corporations or oligarchs.**
[**But we can’t do it without you, Peter. If you believe in that vision, will you chip in $3 today to help us keep building it together?**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/pj-email-20250418-1?refcode=em20250418-4963&amount=3&refcode2=4963_420207_mTEno3&akid=4963%2E420207%2EmTEno3)

Thank you for being part of this movement.
In solidarity,
Team Jayapal

clean_body: At the Bernie rally, Pramila laid out exactly what we’re fighting for. Here’s what she said. Peter, at the Bernie Sanders and AOC rally in Los Angeles, Pramila brought the house down with a message we all need to hear right now: We’re not just an opposition party – we’re a proposition party. While MAGA Republicans push a budget that slashes Medicaid and delivers massive tax breaks to billionaires, we’re fighting for something better : ✅ Universal childcare that’s actually affordable ✅ A healthcare system that ensures everyone can get the care they need without breaking the bank ✅ Fundamental freedoms, from the right to decide what happens to your own body to the right to speak your mind and voice your opinion This isn’t just about resisting the far-right. It’s about advancing a bold, inclusive vision for the future where working people come first, not corporations or oligarchs. Thank you for being part of this movement. In solidarity, Team Jayapal | | Email us: info@pramilaforcongress.com

name: Democratic National Committee
subject: Why your support is so important right now…
body:
Americans did not elect Elon Musk.
---
Derek, We’re about to ask you to donate directly to the DNC to help us build a strong Democratic Party that will fight back against Trump and his extreme billionaire allies. If that’s all you need to hear, you can use this link to pitch in: [**https://democrats.org/go/April**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-april-2025?amount=3&refcode=em_20250418_3224_fr&refcode2=3224_22930333_wzQwLN&akid=3224%2E22930333%2EwzQwLN) Otherwise, we hope you’ll give us a chance to explain why your support is so important right now: Americans did not elect Elon Musk. But every day, he and DOGE wreak havoc on our government — and the rest of us will pay the price. In just a few months, Musk has gutted multiple federal agencies, laying off thousands of employees and putting the sensitive information of millions of people at risk. Musk has targeted agencies like the Internal Revenue Service, Social Security, the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau, and others. Agencies like the CFPB protect consumers. The IRS and Social Security hold sensitive taxpayer data and pay out millions of dollars to Americans every month — money that Americans rely on to live. And the agencies he is targeting are not a coincidence. Many of them have been involved in investigations into Musk and his businesses. It’s clear Elon Musk is more worried about protecting his own interests than the interests of the American people. And Trump is letting him get away with it. What’s happening right now is extraordinarily dangerous. And the consequences of crippling these agencies and allowing Musk unchecked control could be disastrous. **Republicans have already proven that they won’t check Trump or Musk’s power, so we need to elect as many Democrats as possible who will. But to do that, we need your help.** Maybe you weren’t considering making a contribution to the DNC today, but we hope this message has shown you how urgent and meaningful your support is right now. So here comes the part where we ask: [**If you can, make a $3 contribution now to support the DNC’s work to elect Democrats up and down the ballot who will fight back:**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-april-2025?amount=3&refcode=em_20250418_3224_fr&refcode2=3224_22930333_wzQwLN&akid=3224%2E22930333%2EwzQwLN) _If you 've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly._ | | | **[Donate $3](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-april-2025?express_lane=true&amount=3&refcode=em_20250418_3224_fr&refcode2=3224_22930333_wzQwLN&akid=3224%2E22930333%2EwzQwLN)**

name: Eric H. Holder, Jr.
subject: Peter, I rarely write with such urgency
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the National Democratic Redistricting Committee, John Bisognano, President
body:
Together, we can defend our democracy — and we must.
| [](https://click.everyaction.com/k/106095709/540407363/-3547583?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk0IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjVkNDEzNjFiLWE3MWMtZjAxMS04YjNkLTAwMjI0ODJhOWZiNyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=Maar6f0OoA7tgY3OAlBc-i98Ui9URHNJvCJdO2ICOgg=&emci=95d590a6-981c-f011-8b3d-0022482a9fb7&emdi=5d41361b-a71c-f011-8b3d-0022482a9fb7&ceid=8431045)
---

Peter, I rarely write with such urgency, but what is happening in North Carolina right now should alarm every American who believes in democracy and the rule of law. Late last week, the North Carolina Supreme Court issued an outrageous ruling throwing out legally cast ballots from overseas voters and specifically targeting military members from Democratic counties. This ruling requires them to once again prove their identities within a narrow 30-day window to ensure the votes they cast in November are counted. **Let me be absolutely clear: This enables a naked attempt to steal an election.** [**And we must all come together to reject these kinds of blatant attacks on our democracy.**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/106095712/540407366/1925646595?refcode=don_ndrc_tier1_email_AGNC_email_20250418-NDRC-Tier1-AGNC_31&utm_source=email&utm_medium=email&utm_term=don&utm_content=20250418-NDRC-Tier1-AGNC_31&utm_campaign=AGNC&amount=30&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk0IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjVkNDEzNjFiLWE3MWMtZjAxMS04YjNkLTAwMjI0ODJhOWZiNyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=Maar6f0OoA7tgY3OAlBc-i98Ui9URHNJvCJdO2ICOgg=) As a former Attorney General who made protecting voting rights a top priority at the Justice Department, I am appalled to see the judiciary abandon its most fundamental responsibility — protecting the rights of the people. Too many members of the Republican majority on North Carolina's highest court cherry-picked whose votes count based purely on partisan calculations. It is the height of political arrogance to tell military members who serve and sacrifice for our country, and other voters, that their votes and those of their family members are somehow questionable. The facts of this case aren't complicated, Peter. Multiple recounts, the state Board of Elections, and the trial court all concluded that Justice Riggs was re-elected in November. Like North Carolina Supreme Court Justice Anita Earls powerfully stated in her dissenting opinion: "It is no small thing to overturn the results of an election in a democracy by throwing out ballots that were legally cast consistent with all election laws in effect on the day of the election... Some would call it stealing the election, others might call it a bloodless coup, but by whatever name, no amount of smoke and mirrors makes it legitimate." **This case reminds us how every generation of Americans is ultimately called upon to defend democracy — to bring our nation closer to its founding ideals._Now it is our time._** It is only when the times are toughest that we are truly tested. Rest assured that we, like those before us, are up to this test. The tasks before us will not be easily accomplished. In the first year of our independence Tom Paine said, “tyranny, like hell, is not easily conquered; yet we have this consolation with us, that the harder the conflict, the more glorious the triumph. What we obtain too cheap we esteem too lightly; it is dearness only that gives everything its value.” **Together, we can defend our democracy — _and we must_.** [**As the NDRC continues to fight for free and fair elections in North Carolina and around the country, please make a donation of $31 or more today to help power this work.**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/106095715/540407368/1925646595?refcode=don_ndrc_tier1_email_AGNC_email_20250418-NDRC-Tier1-AGNC_31&utm_source=email&utm_medium=email&utm_term=don&utm_content=20250418-NDRC-Tier1-AGNC_31&utm_campaign=AGNC&amount=31&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk0IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjVkNDEzNjFiLWE3MWMtZjAxMS04YjNkLTAwMjI0ODJhOWZiNyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=Maar6f0OoA7tgY3OAlBc-i98Ui9URHNJvCJdO2ICOgg=) Thank you, Eric H. Holder, Jr.
82nd Attorney General of the United States

clean_body: Together, we can defend our democracy — and we must. | Peter, I rarely write with such urgency, but what is happening in North Carolina right now should alarm every American who believes in democracy and the rule of law. Late last week, the North Carolina Supreme Court issued an outrageous ruling throwing out legally cast ballots from overseas voters and specifically targeting military members from Democratic counties. This ruling requires them to once again prove their identities within a narrow 30-day window to ensure the votes they cast in November are counted. Let me be absolutely clear: This enables a naked attempt to steal an election. = As a former Attorney General who made protecting voting rights a top priority at the Justice Department, I am appalled to see the judiciary abandon its most fundamental responsibility — protecting the rights of the people. Too many members of the Republican majority on North Carolina's highest court cherry-picked whose votes count based purely on partisan calculations. It is the height of political arrogance to tell military members who serve and sacrifice for our country, and other voters, that their votes and those of their family members are somehow questionable. The facts of this case aren't complicated, Peter. Multiple recounts, the state Board of Elections, and the trial court all concluded that Justice Riggs was re-elected in November. Like North Carolina Supreme Court Justice Anita Earls powerfully stated in her dissenting opinion: "It is no small thing to overturn the results of an election in a democracy by throwing out ballots that were legally cast consistent with all election laws in effect on the day of the election... Some would call it stealing the election, others might call it a bloodless coup, but by whatever name, no amount of smoke and mirrors makes it legitimate." This case reminds us how every generation of Americans is ultimately called upon to defend democracy — to bring our nation closer to its founding ideals.Now it is our time. It is only when the times are toughest that we are truly tested. Rest assured that we, like those before us, are up to this test. The tasks before us will not be easily accomplished. In the first year of our independence Tom Paine said, “tyranny, like hell, is not easily conquered; yet we have this consolation with us, that the harder the conflict, the more glorious the triumph. What we obtain too cheap we esteem too lightly; it is dearness only that gives everything its value.” Together, we can defend our democracy — and we must. = Thank you, Eric H. Holder, Jr. 82nd Attorney General of the United States Chairman, NDRC | = = Since 2017, the NDRC has executed a comprehensive redistricting strategy that shifts the redistricting power, creating fair districts where Democrats can compete. Our victories have been made possible by our strong community of grassroots supporters. = If you want to receive periodic updates from the NDRC on our fight for fair maps, text MAPS to 36787. Text HELP for help, STOP to end. Msg & Data rates may apply. We know we send a lot of emails, but we think it's important to keep you updated with the latest information on Republican gerrymandering and voter suppression and how we're fighting back -- regardless of whether you choose to donate. = to make a donation. Every dollar you give will go toward our fight to end gerrymandering and voter suppression. | | Paid for by the National Democratic Redistricting Committee, John Bisognano, President. | | | | | ---|---|---|---|--- National Democratic Redistricting Committee 712 H St NE, #25 Washington, DC 20002 United States if you'd like to receive fewer emails from the NDRC.

name: Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez
subject: Everything feels increasingly like a scam
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress
body:
Our government can be tremendously efficient for the wealthy, but inefficient in delivering things for working people.
#
Our society is slanted so that the gains go to the wealthy, the corporate class, and the largest corporations in America who pay the least in taxes.
And everyday working people, **who pay much higher percentages of their income in taxes** , see the _least_ benefit in society.
That is a _governance_ problem, it is a _social_ problem, it is an _economy-wide_ problem.
I think a boiling rage exists at this sense of injustice that you are working 1-2 jobs, scared to go to the doctor, and not even making $8-$9 in a lot of places in this country.
And on top of that, **everything feels increasingly like a scam**. Not only are grocery prices going up, but everything has a fee and a surcharge.
In terms of efficiency — our government can be tremendously efficient for the wealthy, but inefficient in delivering things for working people.
**That is not the same thing as justifying cutting the very few things that people actually interface with** : going after the post office, going after the VA, going after Medicaid and Medicare.
Our movement for real progress for everyday people will have to push harder than ever these next four years to **stop Trump and his billionaire donors from making the country into their personal slush fund**.
[Help us continue the fight with a grassroots contribution today.](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/14659?c0=aoc-email-20250418-2.iK7rum&refcode=em20250418-8644-P_FND_EGR_GRM&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1&akid=8644%2E2526796%2EF3hrvU)
**[Contribute $5](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/14659?c0=aoc-email-20250418-2.iK7rum&refcode=em20250418-8644-P_FND_EGR_GRM&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=20&akid=8644%2E2526796%2EF3hrvU)**
In solidarity,
Alexandria

**[Donate](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/aoc-email-20250418-2?refcode=em20250418-8644-P_FND_EGR_GRM&t=21&refcode2=8644_2526796_F3hrvU&akid=8644%2E2526796%2EF3hrvU)** | **[Merch](https://shop.ocasiocortez.com/?utm_source=em-footer-20250418-8644-P_FND_EGR_GRM&t=22&akid=8644%2E2526796%2EF3hrvU)**

name: Tom Fitton
subject: Biden WH Cover-Up Exposed
body:
Happy Easter!

[INSIDE JW]

JUDICIAL WATCH LAWSUIT FORCES RELEASE OF NASHVILLE SCHOOL SHOOTER
“MANIFESTO” RECORDS

[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=73501&L=35011&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly95b3V0dWJlLmNvbS9saXZlL3pTd2w2UFpXNzQ4P3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=91e67ad979ae51cdd1ceb05ab50a096ac8312fddec86ca86d142bdc129c9ed68]

We just received 112 pages
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=73501&L=35008&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL2ZiaS1uYXNodmlsbGUtc2hvb3Rlci1tYW5pZmVzdG8tcHJvZHVjdGlvbi8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=28357fb9cc9e544ee8a473315c5ece8cc7a95903a75ab1da1764c69939fe6124]
of documents of the “manifesto” of the March 27, 2023, shooter at
The Covenant School in Tennessee. The records were an interim release
in response to our FOIA lawsuit (_Judicial Watch v. U.S. Department of
Justice_
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=73501&L=35009&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL2p3LXYtZG9qLWNvbXBsYWludC0wMTQ4My8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=ca0fbafd89ccbe8bd588b8638d651dd1606e4dbe3f71c4f7f27ef109e229e3fc]
(No. 1:23-cv-01483)).

The records detail the shooter’s violent thoughts, the targeting and
planning of the shooting attack on the Covenant School, and
transgender-related distress. The records show she considered an
attack on a mall but did not pursue it because of the facility’s
security measures.

The Covenant School killer’s ‘manifesto’ records are exceedingly
disturbing but should have been released long ago. We appreciate the
Trump administration’s transparency. These records may help
Americans understand and perhaps better prevent and protect their
schools and other targets from mass shooters.

Separately, we filed a lawsuit
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=73501&L=29572&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvY292ZW5hbnQtc2Nob29sLXNob290ZXIvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=6f9aa05e8e79d3ec973023f3c14fff8bfa2b7e52bcca70917c97d3d8be76c7c4]
on behalf of
retired Hamilton County Sheriff James Hammond and the Tennessee
Firearms Association, Inc. (“TFA”) (_Hammond et al. v.
Metropolitan Govt of Nashville et al._
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=73501&L=29435&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL2hhbW1vbmQtdi1uYXNodmlsbGUtcGV0aXRpb24tZm9yLXJlY29yZHMvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=b9c4350b5474c0c620605405737db353f054f8a2566e0e8af4ac12eeec04a44d]
(No. 23-0538-III)). The lawsuit is on appeal after a lower court
upheld efforts to keep all the records secret.

WHITE HOUSE REVISIONS TO BIDEN SPECIAL COUNSEL INTERVIEW TRANSCRIPT
REVEALED

Joe Biden’s handlers, including his White House lawyers, bent over
backwards to prevent the American people from learning that he
wasn’t up to the job.

We’re adding to the evidence.

Judicial Watch received 52 pages
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=73501&L=34998&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL2p3LXYtZG9qLWJpZGVuLWh1ci1pbnRlcnZpZXctcmVjb3Jkcy0wMDcwMC8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=e8dbb3630fe0816433e7c8978877362de587b6edb4ed8e86c6644aae9a6d5d55]
of documents from the U.S. Department of Justice showing White House
staffers suggesting edits to transcripts of President Biden’s
interview with Special Counsel Robert Hur regarding his handling of
secret documents.

We received the documents thanks to our FOIA lawsuit
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=73501&L=33067&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL2p3LXYtZG9qLWh1ci1lZGl0cy1jb21wbGFpbnQtMDIxNzYvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=81723dd4a47903007b7bbe102306b41811fc7fdc6b3167f28a70876c48731e45]
against the Department of Justice for records of communication between
the agency and White House regarding the altered transcripts of
Special Counsel Robert Hur’s October 2023 interviews of President
Biden in the criminal investigation into Biden’s theft and
disclosure of classified records (_Judicial Watch v. U.S. Department
of Justice_
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=73501&L=33067&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL2p3LXYtZG9qLWh1ci1lZGl0cy1jb21wbGFpbnQtMDIxNzYvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=81723dd4a47903007b7bbe102306b41811fc7fdc6b3167f28a70876c48731e45]
(No. 1:24-cv-02176)).

In a separate lawsuit brought by Judicial Watch, a federal
court ordered
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=73501&L=34522&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL2p3LXYtZG9qLWJpZGVuLWh1ci1vcmRlci0wMDcwMC8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=dc75c7d09ffb4bea1eab2a27358180d2011963f07a9ccf08b455be7f4e38b7bb]
the Department of Justice to declare whether it intends to continue
denying Judicial Watch’s request for the full audio of former
President Joe Biden’s interview with Special Counsel Robert Hur. The
Trump Justice Department has until May 20, 2025, to report its
position on the release of the videotape. (_Judicial Watch, Inc. v.
U.S. Department of Justice_
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=73501&L=31585&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL2p3LXYtZG9qLWJpZGVuLWh1ci1pbnRlcnZpZXctY29tcGxhaW50LTAwNzAwLz91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9QU1qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNEJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=&hash=6dc84b815e33e4d79cc93a6ce0254c139b20bb5b159e92efdbcbf14e22ef5017]
(No. 1:24-cv-00700))

Also, previously in that lawsuit, we forced
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=73501&L=32576&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvY291bnNlbC10cmFuc2NyaXB0LWluYWNjdXJhdGUvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=7f63dd32df079828f99b4c52dd105cde3e7bbee5d4c80730fc637c6075c738ee]
the
Biden administration to confess that the transcripts of the audio
recordings have been altered and are not accurate.

The new documents provided in this case include a chart of suggested
changes by Biden White House and personal lawyers to the transcript.
Also included are emails that detail Biden lawyer Bob Bauer’s
requesting access to interview exhibits and a meeting with the special
counsel to discuss the case. Justice Department official Marc
Krickbaum confirmed most White House revisions to the transcript were
accepted, including minor clarifications and changes potentially
masking Biden’s confusion, such as correcting who said “Yeah” or
altering references to Biden’s Delaware garage and President
Biden’s seeming inability to recall the name of the Defense
Secretary.

These documents provide an extraordinary insight into a cover-up of
the White House of Biden’s cognitive challenges. The Bondi Justice
Department should follow up with the full release of the actual audio
of President Biden’s disastrous interview with the special counsel
on his document theft and mishandling.

On October 12, 2023, Bob Bauer, personal attorney to Biden and White
House Counsel for President Obama, writes
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=73501&L=34999&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL2p3LXYtZG9qLWJpZGVuLWh1ci1pbnRlcnZpZXctcmVjb3Jkcy0wMDcwMC1wZy05Lz91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9QU1qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNEJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=&hash=ce398cfba4293d7c01e937dd844e78c16315f49c3d8f2bef05d13d34257a0e99]
to Marc Krickbaum, then-Deputy Special Counsel, and others, including
several White House staffers:

> Gentlemen:
>
> Now that the interviews of the President have concluded, we would
> like to discuss with you the written presentations that we are
> preparing to aid in the resolution of this matter, as well as the
> schedule for their timely submission. To this end, we request a
> meeting for the purpose of hearing from you where matters stand in
> the case, which would enable us to focus our presentations on the
> issues it would be most helpful for us to address. The meeting would
> include both personal and White House Counsel.
>
> ***
>
> As you know, we also have an outstanding request for copies of the
> exhibits you provided in your interview of the President. We have
> noted that these exhibits would be necessary in our review of the
> transcripts of the interviews to check for any omissions or
> inaccuracies. We believe it is fair that we have access to this
> material at least for this purpose. This is one of the issues we
> would like to resolve at the meeting, along with an understanding
> for our planning purposes of when the interview transcript may be
> available for review in both audio and video form.

In a follow-up email, he corrects himself: “...the reference to
‘video’ can, of course, be disregarded.”

On December 21, 2023, Krickbaum writes
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=73501&L=35000&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL2p3LXYtZG9qLWJpZGVuLWh1ci1pbnRlcnZpZXctcmVjb3Jkcy0wMDcwMC1wZy0xNS8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=07c518c3a88eaf8f409fc728ac31a6e94b630ac3ce22abb93d7865c06081fc4b]
to Rachel F. Cotton, Deputy Counsel to the President in the Office of
the White House Counsel, and others showing the court reporter
rejected some of the proposed edits to the Biden transcript

> Dick and Rachel,
>
> We had the court reporter who prepared the transcript review your
> suggested revisions. She accepted most of them. This table lists
> your revisions and then describes whether we did or did not make
> changes.
>
> We may have to send the transcript itself on the high side
> [classified] next week. I will let you know when we do.
>
> Thank you.
>
> Marc

Some proposed transcript edits detailed
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=73501&L=35001&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL2p3LXYtZG9qLWJpZGVuLWh1ci1pbnRlcnZpZXctcmVjb3Jkcy0wMDcwMC1wZy0yMS8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=9195f035d14cb29c239ed54248217ffb855ff9b6bc442b2ea6c966723abb560c]
in a chart produced by the Justice Department suggest that the Biden
White House changes to the transcript might be considered an effort to
cover up Biden’s mental confusion. White House suggests:

> PRESIDENT BIDEN: The date is 4-20-09. Was I still Vice President? I
> was, wasn’t I? Yeah. Yeah.
>
> Mr. Bauer: Yeah.
>
> President Biden: Yeah.

On this entry, the White House attaches a note: “Audio indicates
that ‘Yeah’ was said twice in succession by President Biden. Mr.
Bauer did not say ‘Yeah.’

The court reporter records:

> PRESIDENT BIDEN: The date is 4-20-09. Was I still Vice President? I
> was, wasn’t I? Yeah. Yeah.
>
> UNIDENTIFIED MALE: Yeah.
>
> PRESIDENT BIDEN: Yeah.

Other changes also seem significant. One seems to refer

DID DEMOCRAT ARIZONA AG ANNOUNCE TRUMP ‘INVESTIGATION’ TO SWAY

Here is yet another example of “lawfare” abuse targeting Donald
Trump.

Our open records lawsuit reveals that Arizona Attorney General Kris
Mayes seems to have used her office for political purposes in
threatening a prosecution of President Trump on the eve of the 2024
presidential election (_Judicial Watch Inc. v. Kris Mayes et al_
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=73501&L=34990&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL2p3LXYta3Jpcy1tYXllcy1ldC1hbC1hei10cnVtcC1jb21tZW50LWludmVzdGlnYXRpb24tY29tcGxhaW50LTAwMDY3NT91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9QU1qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNEJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=&hash=f5db2a327608a2ccfc9953f70f24c351a126d94315aeafb88d21eabeafac5b7e]
(No. CV 2025 00675)). Only one document was found (and kept secret)
relating to a criminal investigation, while dozens of
media-related documents
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=73501&L=34985&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL21heWVzLXJlcXVlc3QtZm9yLWNvbW1lbnQtbWVkaWEtb3V0bGV0cy8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=16910e71dbd342890ff7396321fc993b5f086fc516cf19504276c052cc2ca158]
were revealed.

In an October 31, 2024, interview
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=73501&L=34986&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuY2JzbmV3cy5jb20vbmV3cy90cnVtcC1hdHRhY2tzLWxpei1jaGVuZXktd2FyLWhhd2stZ3Vucy10cmFpbmVkLW9uLWhlci1mYWNlLz91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9QU1qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNEJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=&hash=d26dec9014689821a0a0ac446ac11519b02bfe22abf3028ae6888b99c0519d36]
with Tucker Carlson at Desert Diamond Arena in Glendale, Arizona,
Trump said of Liz Cheney: “She’s a radical war hawk. Let’s put
her with a rifle standing there with nine barrels shooting at her, OK?
Let’s see how she feels about it. You know, when the guns are
trained on her face. They’re all war hawks when they’re sitting in
Washington in a nice building saying, ‘Oh, gee, well let’s send
10,000 troops right into the mouth of the enemy.’ “

On November 1, 2024, Mayes, a Democrat, said
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=73501&L=34987&F=T&&hash=8547b15ebfd390768cc62e26b100a8583c0753fa2c6584d164747d19b3d7a7b2]
during the taping of the Channel 12 “Sunday Square-Off” news show:
“I have already asked my criminal division chief to start looking at
that statement, analyzing it for whether it qualifies as a death
threat under Arizona’s laws.” She conducted multiple media
interviews within days of the election.

Mayes’ office provided comments
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=73501&L=34985&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL21heWVzLXJlcXVlc3QtZm9yLWNvbW1lbnQtbWVkaWEtb3V0bGV0cy8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=16910e71dbd342890ff7396321fc993b5f086fc516cf19504276c052cc2ca158]
to CBS, NBC, CNN, AZ Family, Forbes, Fox, News Nation, Sky View
Networks, Law and Crime News Network, Newsweek, and Reuters in what
appears to be an attempt to paint Donald Trump as a criminal shortly
before the election.

The records show the threatened “investigation” of President Trump
was merely an oral request from the Arizona General Mayes to a top
staffer to evaluate the Liz Cheney statement. The only record of the
investigation that exists is a 3-page memo from the Attorney
General’s criminal division chief back to Mayes, which the Court
reviewed _in camera_ and determined was protected attorney work
product and therefore can be withheld. Presumably (and probably from
media sources) the memo declined to open a more substantial
investigation or initiate a prosecution.

The lack of records further supports our theory that the
investigation—purportedly launched on the Friday before the 2024
presidential election and dropped shortly thereafter—was a sham to
try to influence the outcome of the election in an important swing
state.

Mayes ended the “investigation” on November 13, 2024, telling the
Arizona Republic that Trump’s comment “very likely may have been
an effort to intimidate Cheney” but the investigation showed there
was “no reasonable likelihood that we could obtain a conviction for
Trump’s statements. We think it’s equally likely a reasonable
person could conclude Trump was discussing war, and Liz Cheney not
wanting to go to war.”

We filed our lawsuit in the Superior Court for the State of Arizona,
Maricopa County, this past January after Mayes’ office failed to
comply with a November 12, 2024, Arizona Public Records Act request
for records regarding Mayes’ office and/or the criminal division
chief analyzing Trump’s statement as qualifying as a death threat;
the determination whether the statement was analyzed to be a violation
of Arizona and/or federal law; the costs to carry out the
investigation; and any documents in which Mayes addresses the limits
of free speech as addressed in the First Amendment.

(Additionally, we asked for records regarding the dismissal of
criminal charges
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=73501&L=34988&F=T&&hash=7b3dd0a4c2494a48d6cb0fd1425d885f8459faaaf21c5b0184df11e025a22f7f]
against Arizona citizen Rebekah Massie, who was arrested during an
August 20, 2024, Surprise City Council meeting after she criticized a
proposed pay increase for the city attorney.

Maricopa County Judge Gerald Williams dismissed with prejudice the
trespassing charge against Massie, writing: “No branch of any
federal, state, or local government in this country should ever
attempt to control the content of political speech. In this case, the
government did so in a manner that was objectively outrageous.”)

clean_body: Happy Easter! [INSIDE JW] JUDICIAL WATCH LAWSUIT FORCES RELEASE OF NASHVILLE SCHOOL SHOOTER “MANIFESTO” RECORDS [] We just received 112 pages [] of documents of the “manifesto” of the March 27, 2023, shooter at The Covenant School in Tennessee. The records were an interim release in response to our FOIA lawsuit (Judicial Watch v. U.S. Department of Justice [] (No. 1:23-cv-01483)). The records detail the shooter’s violent thoughts, the targeting and planning of the shooting attack on the Covenant School, and transgender-related distress. The records show she considered an attack on a mall but did not pursue it because of the facility’s security measures. The Covenant School killer’s ‘manifesto’ records are exceedingly disturbing but should have been released long ago. We appreciate the Trump administration’s transparency. These records may help Americans understand and perhaps better prevent and protect their schools and other targets from mass shooters. Separately, we filed a lawsuit [] on behalf of retired Hamilton County Sheriff James Hammond and the Tennessee Firearms Association, Inc. (“TFA”) (Hammond et al. v. Metropolitan Govt of Nashville et al. [] (No. 23-0538-III)). The lawsuit is on appeal after a lower court upheld efforts to keep all the records secret. WHITE HOUSE REVISIONS TO BIDEN SPECIAL COUNSEL INTERVIEW TRANSCRIPT REVEALED Joe Biden’s handlers, including his White House lawyers, bent over backwards to prevent the American people from learning that he wasn’t up to the job. We’re adding to the evidence. Judicial Watch received 52 pages [] of documents from the U.S. Department of Justice showing White House staffers suggesting edits to transcripts of President Biden’s interview with Special Counsel Robert Hur regarding his handling of secret documents. We received the documents thanks to our FOIA lawsuit [] against the Department of Justice for records of communication between the agency and White House regarding the altered transcripts of Special Counsel Robert Hur’s October 2023 interviews of President Biden in the criminal investigation into Biden’s theft and disclosure of classified records (Judicial Watch v. U.S. Department of Justice [] (No. 1:24-cv-02176)). In a separate lawsuit brought by Judicial Watch, a federal court ordered [] the Department of Justice to declare whether it intends to continue denying Judicial Watch’s request for the full audio of former President Joe Biden’s interview with Special Counsel Robert Hur. The Trump Justice Department has until May 20, 2025, to report its position on the release of the videotape. (Judicial Watch, Inc. v. U.S. Department of Justice [] (No. 1:24-cv-00700)) Also, previously in that lawsuit, we forced [] the Biden administration to confess that the transcripts of the audio recordings have been altered and are not accurate. The new documents provided in this case include a chart of suggested changes by Biden White House and personal lawyers to the transcript. Also included are emails that detail Biden lawyer Bob Bauer’s requesting access to interview exhibits and a meeting with the special counsel to discuss the case. Justice Department official Marc Krickbaum confirmed most White House revisions to the transcript were accepted, including minor clarifications and changes potentially masking Biden’s confusion, such as correcting who said “Yeah” or altering references to Biden’s Delaware garage and President Biden’s seeming inability to recall the name of the Defense Secretary. These documents provide an extraordinary insight into a cover-up of the White House of Biden’s cognitive challenges. The Bondi Justice Department should follow up with the full release of the actual audio of President Biden’s disastrous interview with the special counsel on his document theft and mishandling. On October 12, 2023, Bob Bauer, personal attorney to Biden and White House Counsel for President Obama, writes [] to Marc Krickbaum, then-Deputy Special Counsel, and others, including several White House staffers: Gentlemen: Now that the interviews of the President have concluded, we would like to discuss with you the written presentations that we are preparing to aid in the resolution of this matter, as well as the schedule for their timely submission. To this end, we request a meeting for the purpose of hearing from you where matters stand in the case, which would enable us to focus our presentations on the issues it would be most helpful for us to address. The meeting would include both personal and White House Counsel. *** As you know, we also have an outstanding request for copies of the exhibits you provided in your interview of the President. We have noted that these exhibits would be necessary in our review of the transcripts of the interviews to check for any omissions or inaccuracies. We believe it is fair that we have access to this material at least for this purpose. This is one of the issues we would like to resolve at the meeting, along with an understanding for our planning purposes of when the interview transcript may be available for review in both audio and video form. In a follow-up email, he corrects himself: “...the reference to ‘video’ can, of course, be disregarded.” On December 21, 2023, Krickbaum writes [] to Rachel F. Cotton, Deputy Counsel to the President in the Office of the White House Counsel, and others showing the court reporter rejected some of the proposed edits to the Biden transcript Dick and Rachel, We had the court reporter who prepared the transcript review your suggested revisions. She accepted most of them. This table lists your revisions and then describes whether we did or did not make changes. We may have to send the transcript itself on the high side [classified] next week. I will let you know when we do. Thank you. Marc Some proposed transcript edits detailed [] in a chart produced by the Justice Department suggest that the Biden White House changes to the transcript might be considered an effort to cover up Biden’s mental confusion. White House suggests: PRESIDENT BIDEN: The date is 4-20-09. Was I still Vice President? I was, wasn’t I? Yeah. Yeah. Mr. Bauer: Yeah. President Biden: Yeah. On this entry, the White House attaches a note: “Audio indicates that ‘Yeah’ was said twice in succession by President Biden. Mr. Bauer did not say ‘Yeah.’ The court reporter records: PRESIDENT BIDEN: The date is 4-20-09. Was I still Vice President? I was, wasn’t I? Yeah. Yeah. UNIDENTIFIED MALE: Yeah. PRESIDENT BIDEN: Yeah. Other changes also seem significant. One seems to refer [] to Biden’s garage at his Delaware home, where secret documents were found. The White House proposed this change: were delivered my the garage The court reporter records: were delivered to the – my garage. At another point, Bien might have been confused about the name of the Secretary of Defense. The White House proposed [ change: Secretary of Defense? Gates UNIDENTIFIED MALE SPEAKER: Gates. A White House entry on that notes states: According to the audio, President Biden says “Gates” prior to the unidentified male speaker. The court reporter records: Secretary of Defense? Gates? UNIDENTIFIED MALE SPEAKER: Gates. The White House responds: The court reporter inserted a question mark that is unsupported by the audio recording. It is clear that the President said “Gates” as a statement rather than a question. We ask that the question mark be replaced with a period ... Regarding that last entry and another, on January 3, 2024, Amish [probably Amish Shah, Senior Associate Counsel in the White house Counsel’s Office], emails [] Krickbaum: Thank you for sending. We have reviewed and are fine with almost all of the court reporter’s responses. However, we ask the SCO to reconsider the attached two corrections on Day 2. Both corrections are supported by the audio recording of the interview. On January 22, 2024, Krickbaum replies [ We will send a revised transcript of the President’s interview on yellow [higher security], and attached is a revised list of edits. For the two revisions you suggested in your last email, the court reporter adopted the second and adopted a slightly modified version of the first. On February 28, 2024, an Executive Officer in the Special Counsel’s Office emails [] Free State Reporting: Good evening. Would you be able to come back (hopefully, the last time) on Friday, 3/1 or Monday, 3/4 (the sooner, the better? There are more edits to the President’s transcript. I’ll have an exact number tomorrow. I’m getting the computer back so it will be blank when you arrive so it will need your software on it again. It got wiped when I thought everything was finished. I’ll meet you out front with a new parking pass. My apologies for the inconvenience. It’s not clear what these last-minute changes were. We have several ongoing FOIA lawsuits about Biden’s document scandals and the related unprecedented partisan prosecutorial and judicial abuses of former President Donald J. Trump. I will have updates for you as events warrant! DID DEMOCRAT ARIZONA AG ANNOUNCE TRUMP ‘INVESTIGATION’ TO SWAY ELECTION? Here is yet another example of “lawfare” abuse targeting Donald Trump. Our open records lawsuit reveals that Arizona Attorney General Kris Mayes seems to have used her office for political purposes in threatening a prosecution of President Trump on the eve of the 2024 presidential election (Judicial Watch Inc. v. Kris Mayes et al [] (No. CV 2025 00675)). Only one document was found (and kept secret) relating to a criminal investigation, while dozens of media-related documents [] were revealed. In an October 31, 2024, interview [] with Tucker Carlson at Desert Diamond Arena in Glendale, Arizona, Trump said of Liz Cheney: “She’s a radical war hawk. Let’s put her with a rifle standing there with nine barrels shooting at her, OK? Let’s see how she feels about it. You know, when the guns are trained on her face. They’re all war hawks when they’re sitting in Washington in a nice building saying, ‘Oh, gee, well let’s send 10,000 troops right into the mouth of the enemy.’ “ On November 1, 2024, Mayes, a Democrat, said [] during the taping of the Channel 12 “Sunday Square-Off” news show: “I have already asked my criminal division chief to start looking at that statement, analyzing it for whether it qualifies as a death threat under Arizona’s laws.” She conducted multiple media interviews within days of the election. Mayes’ office provided comments [] to CBS, NBC, CNN, AZ Family, Forbes, Fox, News Nation, Sky View Networks, Law and Crime News Network, Newsweek, and Reuters in what appears to be an attempt to paint Donald Trump as a criminal shortly before the election. The records show the threatened “investigation” of President Trump was merely an oral request from the Arizona General Mayes to a top staffer to evaluate the Liz Cheney statement. The only record of the investigation that exists is a 3-page memo from the Attorney General’s criminal division chief back to Mayes, which the Court reviewed in camera and determined was protected attorney work product and therefore can be withheld. Presumably (and probably from media sources) the memo declined to open a more substantial investigation or initiate a prosecution. The lack of records further supports our theory that the investigation—purportedly launched on the Friday before the 2024 presidential election and dropped shortly thereafter—was a sham to try to influence the outcome of the election in an important swing state. Mayes ended the “investigation” on November 13, 2024, telling the Arizona Republic that Trump’s comment “very likely may have been an effort to intimidate Cheney” but the investigation showed there was “no reasonable likelihood that we could obtain a conviction for Trump’s statements. We think it’s equally likely a reasonable person could conclude Trump was discussing war, and Liz Cheney not wanting to go to war.” We filed our lawsuit in the Superior Court for the State of Arizona, Maricopa County, this past January after Mayes’ office failed to comply with a November 12, 2024, Arizona Public Records Act request for records regarding Mayes’ office and/or the criminal division chief analyzing Trump’s statement as qualifying as a death threat; the determination whether the statement was analyzed to be a violation of Arizona and/or federal law; the costs to carry out the investigation; and any documents in which Mayes addresses the limits of free speech as addressed in the First Amendment. (Additionally, we asked for records regarding the dismissal of criminal charges [] against Arizona citizen Rebekah Massie, who was arrested during an August 20, 2024, Surprise City Council meeting after she criticized a proposed pay increase for the city attorney. Maricopa County Judge Gerald Williams dismissed with prejudice the trespassing charge against Massie, writing: “No branch of any federal, state, or local government in this country should ever attempt to control the content of political speech. In this case, the government did so in a manner that was objectively outrageous.”) PUBLIC PRE-K-8 “INCLUSIVITY” BOOKS PUSH GENDER TRANSITIONING, DRAG QUEENS Public schools in Maryland, right next door to the nation’s capital, are definitely promoting left-wing extremist and anti-American ideas to children as young as pre-kindergarten. Our Corruption Chronicles blog has the details []. Leftwing activism has run amok in two Maryland public school districts with “inclusivity” books for elementary students that champion gender transitioning, drag queens and children’s pronoun preferences and a high school student suspended for questioning why all his classrooms do not have an American flag as state law requires. The taxpayer-funded districts are situated about an hour apart but practice the same woke ideology that has gripped academic institutions throughout the United States, a leftist conditioning that President Trump has vowed to abolish. An executive order [] issued in late January states that federal funding and support will be eliminated for indoctrination in K-12 schools, including based on gender ideology and discriminatory equity ideology and that parental rights will be protected. “Young men and women are made to question whether they were born in the wrong body and whether to view their parents and their reality as enemies to be blamed,” Trump’s order reads. “These practices not only erode critical thinking but also sow division, confusion, and distrust, which undermine the very foundations of personal identity and family unity.” The order’s language is especially relevant to the first case, which involves Maryland’s largest school district,Montgomery County Public Schools [] (MCPS), with about 160,000 students in 210 campuses located a short distance from Washington D.C. In 2022 the Montgomery County Board of Education announced that students in pre-K through eighth grade would use over 20 new “inclusivity” books that promote gay pride parades, gender transitioning and pronoun preferences for kids. One book directs three and four-year-olds to search for images from a list of words that includes intersex flag, drag queen and underwear as well as the name of a celebrated LGBTQ sex worker turned activist. Other books promote gender transitioning for children, stating that it does not have to make sense and that doctors only guess when identifying a newborn’s gender. When education officials announced the district would use the pride storybooks, it assured concerned parents they would be notified so they could opt out their children. A year later the policy changed, so parents would no longer be warned. A group of Muslim, Catholic, Protestant, and Orthodox Christian parents sued [], and a federal district court and a court of appeals ruled against them so they petitioned the U.S. Supreme Court, which agreed earlier this year to hear their case. A Washington D.C. nonprofit dedicated to protecting the free expression of all faiths represents the concerned parents and will argue before the nation’s highest court on April 22. Upholding parental rights meant that children would not be subjected to age-inappropriate instruction against their parents’ wishes, but the policy change denies parents the right to decide when their elementary-aged children are exposed to books promoting transgender and queer ideology, attorneys for the group say. “The Board cannot refuse parents who want to opt their children out of instruction that violates their religious beliefs on sensitive matters,” the nonprofit representing the parents further points out, adding that it is “unlawfully coming between parents and their kids and targeting them because of their religious beliefs about gender and sexuality.” That violates both Maryland law and the school board’s own policies as well as the U.S. Constitution, the religious freedom charity asserts. In the other case, a high school student about an hour away in Baltimore County was suspended for seven days over his patriotism. The 18-year-old, Parker Jensen, who has enlisted in the military, is a senior at Towson High School and got punished for questioning why all the classrooms in his campus do not have an American flag even though Maryland law says every classroom must have one. When school administrators failed to provide an explanation, Jensen drove to the Baltimore County Board of Education headquarters to ask about the flag violation and district officials called the police on him, according to a local news report []. The high school senior was subsequently suspended for seven days. This month Jensen filed a lawsuit against Baltimore County Public Schools claiming that the district violated his Constitutional rights when it suspended him for inquiring about missing American flags in public school classrooms. “He was summarily suspended without any due process whatsoever, which every student in Baltimore County and Maryland has the right to and they stripped him of that within five seconds,” said Jensen’s attorney, who assures the student’s Constitutional rights were violated. HAPPY EASTER! “Easter says you can put truth in a grave, but it won’t stay there.” ~ Clarence W. Hall In this season, Christians around the world are celebrating the resurrection of Christ. There are no more powerful symbols of hope than the cross and the empty tomb. From me and mine, I wish you and yours all the joy of Easter! For those celebrating Passover, I wish you a Happy Passover, as well! ---
_Democrat Eric Swalwell represents California's 14th Congressional District. _
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/106097184/540412193/-1590518309?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDMvMS85MDcyNyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwMWJlYjg2Yy1hZjFjLWYwMTEtOGIzZC0wMDIyNDgyYTlmYjciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=T0k-Xfqq-8ITNXNim0RbQ78GmenSd_-0Hr90cMiAuKc=&emci=b9edad82-841c-f011-8b3d-0022482a9fb7&emdi=01beb86c-af1c-f011-8b3d-0022482a9fb7&ceid=7878003)

Dear Peter, Yesterday I joined the [Move Oregon coalition and a hundred advocates in the Oregon State Capitol](https://click.everyaction.com/k/106097235/540412355/1756657029?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9BVi9BVkxDVi8xLzcwODc3IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjk5NGM4NjUyLWIwMWMtZjAxMS04YjNkLTAwMjI0ODJhOWZiNyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=-1PU8YfEQS8wFcmflfQGxNMmmhlYuINRKleMwzxr4Z4=&emci=ddc3ba6b-8a1c-f011-8b3d-0022482a9fb7&emdi=994c8652-b01c-f011-8b3d-0022482a9fb7&ceid=2107230) to rally for a safety first, climate forward, and accountable transportation package. Oregonians deserve an equitable transportation system that’s accessible, multimodal, climate-forward, and safe. Access to public transportation is a life-changer: resulting in cleaner air, higher public safety, access to physical activity, and healthier and more vibrant communities. Lawmakers are currently considering how we fund our transportation system, so we spent the day in the Capitol yesterday, advocating for transportation funding that delivers on our safety and climate goals while expanding financially sustainable and well-maintained transportation choices. Despite the support we’ve seen for this package, we know this legislation will be hard to pass.** **[**Will you send your legislators a message in support of safe, climate-forward, and accessible transportation?**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/106097236/540412356/921018097?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9BVi9BVkxDVi8xLzcwODc3IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjk5NGM4NjUyLWIwMWMtZjAxMS04YjNkLTAwMjI0ODJhOWZiNyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=-1PU8YfEQS8wFcmflfQGxNMmmhlYuINRKleMwzxr4Z4=&emci=ddc3ba6b-8a1c-f011-8b3d-0022482a9fb7&emdi=994c8652-b01c-f011-8b3d-0022482a9fb7&ceid=2107230)

All Oregonians deserve clean air, healthy, vibrant communities, and a chance to thrive. As Indi Namkoong, Verde’s transportation justice coordinator, stated on the steps of the Capitol yesterday: “We’ve been ready for a transportation system that offers us real choices. We want the system to be safe, sustainable, well maintained and resilient to the challenges of the future.”

| | The opposition we’ve seen to the [framework](https://click.everyaction.com/k/106097240/540412360/-948600289?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9BVi9BVkxDVi8xLzcwODc3IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjk5NGM4NjUyLWIwMWMtZjAxMS04YjNkLTAwMjI0ODJhOWZiNyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=-1PU8YfEQS8wFcmflfQGxNMmmhlYuINRKleMwzxr4Z4=&emci=ddc3ba6b-8a1c-f011-8b3d-0022482a9fb7&emdi=994c8652-b01c-f011-8b3d-0022482a9fb7&ceid=2107230) that was released in early April, is related to the tax increases that will come with the passage of this transportation package. However, as we’re ready to support funding ideas that reflect our values, we also want to assure that they are fair and don’t add to the burden working people already face. We need a system that works for everyone and is fair - which is exactly what we are advocating for - and we need it now. If we don’t pass this transportation package now, critical programs will lose funding, meaning Oregonians will continue to face record injuries and deaths because they lack safe routes, drivers will face unsafe road conditions, and public transit will be unreliable. Now is our opportunity to pass a life-changing package for Oregonians. It’s time to create a world where every Oregonian has clean air, healthy, vibrant communities, and the chance to thrive. Oregonians deserve change and an accessible transportation system that works for everyone, now. [**Urge your legislators to support a transportation package that reflects our values today!**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/106097241/540412361/921018097?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9BVi9BVkxDVi8xLzcwODc3IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjk5NGM4NjUyLWIwMWMtZjAxMS04YjNkLTAwMjI0ODJhOWZiNyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=-1PU8YfEQS8wFcmflfQGxNMmmhlYuINRKleMwzxr4Z4=&emci=ddc3ba6b-8a1c-f011-8b3d-0022482a9fb7&emdi=994c8652-b01c-f011-8b3d-0022482a9fb7&ceid=2107230)
